events.id: The Spiral Spectacular-Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: The Spiral Spectacular
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Join Steam Incorporated and discover what the Central Plateau has on offer. Experience the Raurimu Spiral first hand, hauled by our beautifully restored diesel locomotive, Da 1410.
Board this special train journey at Paekakariki, Paraparaumu, Waikanae, Otaki, Levin, Palmerston North, Feilding or Marton!
Upon completion the NIMT became important to New Zealand’s economic development, and was also socially significant. The Raurimu Spiral is considered a feat of engineering ingenuity and the success of this solution contributed to the on-going effectiveness of the NIMT. Despite advances in technology and materials in the century since its completion, no feasible alternative to the Spiral has been found. Therefore, the Raurimu Spiral is a lasting tribute to Holmes’ engineering achievements and is of considerable engineering heritage importance.
Train Fare Pricing:
Adult Fare ex. Kapiti/Horowhenua & Return - $169.00
Child Fare ex. Kapiti/Horowhenua & Return - $139.00
Adult Fare ex. Palmerston North/Feilding/Marton & Return - $139.00
Child Fare ex. Palmerston North/Feilding/Marton & Return - $109.00
We can cater for larger group bookings over 10+ people - these are best dealt with directly through us by emailing admin@steaminc.org.nz or contacting 0800 783 264, for a personalized package!

events.id: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026--41.285826, 174.778333-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026
events.long_description:
Date: Sat 18 & Sun 19 April, 2026
Venue: TSB Arena, Wellington
Opening hours: 10am - 4pm
Tickets: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
The ultimate girls' day out awaits you, filled with shopping, pampering and mouth-watering indulgences, and the chance to win amazing prizes!
Gather your besties and prepare for an unforgettable day at the Wellington Women’s Lifestyle Expo on Sat 18 & Sun 19 April at TSB Arena!
150 COMPANIES
The expo will have something for everyone, featuring over 150 of the best lifestyle companies under one roof, including gourmet food and beverages, fashion and beauty, jewellery, health and wellness, unique gifts, art, homeware, eco-products and much more. It’s the perfect opportunity for a girls’ day out, or to treat yourself to a day leisurely wandering between exhibitors.
GOODIE BAGS
Pre-purchase here: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
Our mystery goodie bags are $20 each and usually include a variety of beauty, skincare, snacks, lifestyle products and more. The contents can vary from bag to bag, adding to the mystery! Available to purchase online with your tickets until the Friday night before the expo, after that (on event weekend) you will only be able to purchase goodie bags at the expo. We recommend pre-purchasing as they often sell out.
SEMINAR SESSIONS
Don’t miss our free Seminar Sessions - happening at 11am, 12pm & 1pm. The Seminar Sessions are located at the end of the first row of the Expo (opposite end to the Expo entry).
WIN A TRIP FOR TWO TO HAWAII
While you're at the Expo, enter the draw to win an incredible Hawaiian Getaway for two (return flights and seven nights accommodation), all thanks to World Travellers and Hawaiian Airlines. T&Cs apply: www.womenslifestyleexpo.co.nz/win
TASTE ZONE
Appreciating that shopping, pampering, trying and testing can be hard work, the expo Taste Zone (a mini food show) will be on hand to offer pick-me-ups, tasty treats and bites to keep you going.
TICKETS
Pre-purchase here: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
Online Pre-Sales (available until Fri 17 Apr):
Earlybird x1 - $9
Earlybird x2 - $16
Child x1 (aged 6-12) - $5
Kids 5 & Under Free
Please note, there will be a small booking fee for online purchases.
DOOR SALES
Adult x1 - $13
Child x1 (6-12) - $6
Kids 5 & Under Free
EXHIBITORS
Keen to exhibit at the expo? Find out more at www.womenslifestyleexpo.co.nz/apply, or call the friendly NZME Events team on 06 354 0498, or email enquire@events.nzme.co.nz
FIND OUT MORE:
www.womenslifestyleexpo.co.nz/wellington

events.id: Saosin 20th Anniversary Self Titled Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Saosin 20th Anniversary Self Titled Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
SAOSIN CELEBRATE 20 YEARS OF THEIR SELF-TITLED ALBUM WITH 2026 NEW ZEALAND TOUR

Supported By: Senses Fail
Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to New Zealand in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits. Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail.

Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go. Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone". The album's success propelled Saosin into mainstream recognition, earning them a dedicated global fanbase and cementing their status as one of the most influential bands of their era. Now, 20 years on they’ll perform their self-titled album in full across New Zealand alongside their biggest hits next April.

Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years. With Reber back at the helm, Saosin is redefining their future. The chemistry among the original lineup has sparked creative momentum, leading to new material, revitalized performances, and expanded vision. The band is once again a driving force in post-hardcore, proving that while time may pass, authenticity and connection endure. This chapter in Saosin’s journey is both a homecoming and a launching point for what’s next.
events.description:
Supported By: Senses Fail Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to New Zealand in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits.
Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail.
Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go.
Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone".
Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years.

events.id: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026
events.long_description:
What’s tough, fast, and physical? Wellington’s netball team, Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse. After reaching the semi-finals last year, the Pulse are hungry to dominate the ANZ Premiership this year. Their campaign kicks off in April and continues through to June. Five of the ten games are played on home soil, either at the TSB Arena on Wellington’s waterfront, Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua or Fly Palmy Arena in Palmerston North. 
Netball is known for its speed, agility, deft footwork, and pinpoint feeds. As a live spectator sport, it always delivers. This year, Khiarna Williams, Ameliaranne Ekenasio and Ine-Marí Venter provide the fireworks at the shooting end of the court. Head court-side to cheer them on and feel the energy pulse.

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Jewellery-making on Monday Nights - 6 weeks block, Level 2+-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Jewellery-making on Monday Nights - 6 weeks block, Level 2+
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our popular weekend block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.
This class is for level 2+ and advanced students. It is not open to beginners.
Students must have completed at least two 4-week blocks or a 6-week fabrication block. If you are unsure of your level because you may have done a few different shorter classes, please contact us and we can advise you.
Students will be supported in further developing their jewellery-making skills in the areas that interest them.
Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chainmaking as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something to hone their skills that they are familiar with, as well as taking on a new skill. Their project could be a pendant on a handmade chain, a charm bracelet or a more complex bezel setting - for example a teardrop or soft square stone to take on bezels with corners!
Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques.
This class fee does NOT include materials for any student. All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!
Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this
Course fee $355, Early bird $325 till 22 March.

events.name: Brazilian Zouk - Improvers - Variations Yoyo and Bonus
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come and join us for Improvers Zouk Block C (5 weeks).
We have some great new content for you this year and will be building on foundational movements.
The complete Improvers course consists of 6 different Blocks (A-F).
Each block focuses on a different aspect and technique of Brazilian Zouk and so they can be completed independently of each other.
Our recommendation is to complete all 6 Blocks before progressing onto the Brazilian Zouk Deep Dive classes.
In Improvers Block B we will introduce you to cool variations on the Yoyo and Bonus Movements, as well as some exercises on responding to extension, compression.
===Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week Block cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students
- Ad hoc cost per class is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25
Details: Your name and ZoukImpC
===Location:
Classes are in Footnote dance studio, 60 Dixon Street
===Housekeeping:
- You should have completed the 4 beginner Class blocks before joining Improvers classes
- Drop in or new students, please get in contact before the start of class to let us know you are interested so we can assess your level and organise a private class if necessary to catch you up. If you just show up on the night, you are welcome to watch the class but will not be able to join in (this is to respect the learning of our regular students as the classes are progressive)

events.id: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block C-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block C
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Connect with other like minded people that have a love of dance.
Brazilian Zouk was developed in Rio de Janeiro in the mid 90s, and is now becoming more and more popular
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfhRHnEAbak
..............
This is stunning to watch and even more fun to dance! It is based on the original Lambada dance but adapted to more modern music with a slow-quick-quick beat. It can be danced to a wide variety of music including R&B, Rock and Pop.
In this block of 5 classes you will discover the fundamental techniques, movements and sequences of Brazilian Zouk to get you up and running on the dance floor.
This includes:
Week 1: Passo basico + elastico push
Week 2: Lateral
Week 3: Elástico upright (lead walking around)
Week 4: Abertura e giro
Week 5: Combination

events.id: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 3-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 3
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The New Zealand warm-up shows precede Bret and Jemaine’s three Southern California appearances in May: Thursday 7th May at the Santa Barbara Bowl, and their two Sold-Out appearances at the 2026 edition of the Netflix Is A Joke Festival at The Greek Theatre in Los Angeles on Saturday 9th May, and Sunday 10th May. These are the Flight of the Conchords' first performances in 10 years.
New Zealanders Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement debuted on HBO in 2005 in an edition of the comedy series “One-Night Stand,” returning to the network in 2007 for the debut season of their acclaimed, Emmy-nominated series “Flight of the Conchords,” which followed fictionalized versions of their lives in New York City.
The duo also signed a worldwide deal with Sub Pop Records for their recorded music, going on to release the Grammy-winning EP, The Distant Future in 2007, followed by two full-lengths and a live album - their self-titled debut in 2008, I Told You I Was Freaky in 2009, and Live In London in 2019. They also headlined in the US and the UK multiple times, including their wildly successful “Flight of the Conchords Sing Flight of the Conchords Tour” in 2016.
The Conchords’ have also enjoyed individual success. Clement is the co-creator (and occasional guest star) of the comedy horror TV shows Wellington Paranormal and What We Do In The Shadows. He had a recurring role in FX’s Legion, and appeared in films like Men In Black 3, The Minecraft Movie, Megan 2.0, and most recently as “Dr. Garvin” in the last two Avatar films (The Way of Water in 2022; and Fire and Ash in 2025).
McKenzie was the songwriter for The Muppets, which won him an Academy Award® for Best Original Song for “Man or Muppet,” and “Muppets Most Wanted.” He's also written songs for TV and film, including The Simpsons and The Minecraft Movie. Bret also recently released two solo albums for Sub Pop - Songs Without Jokes in 2022 and Freak Out City in 2024, and has been touring live in New Zealand, Australia, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm.
In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.
Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.
Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

events.id: Brazilian Zouk - Diamond step - Intermediate-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Brazilian Zouk - Diamond step - Intermediate
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come and join us for Intermediate Zouk Block C (5 weeks).
We have some great new content for you this year and will be building on Improvers movements.
The complete Intermediate course consists of 16 different Blocks (A-P).
Each block focuses on a different aspect and technique of Brazilian Zouk and so they can be completed independently of each other.
In this block we will be focusing on the Diamond step and some damn cool variations, some challenging, some lots of fun
These classes are very Social dance focused, and will give you fantastic knowledge of most movements found on the dance floor.

Details: Your name and ZoukIntC

Classes are in Footnote dance studio, 60 Dixon Street
===Housekeeping:
- You should have completed the 6
Improver Class blocks before joining Intermediate classes
- Drop in or new students, please get in contact before the start of class to let us know you are interested so we can assess your level and organise a private class if necessary to catch you up. If you just show up on the night, you are welcome to watch the class but will not be able to join in (this is to respect the learning of our regular students as the classes are progressive)

events.name: s Wellington Theatre Tours
events.venue: The Opera House Wellington, Wellington, NZ
events.long_description:
General Admission OnlyAll Tickets are Mobile Ticket Only. 
Mobile Tickets are like Print-at-Home tickets but instead of having to print off the tickets yourself, you can just show the barcode on your mobile phone. It is the easiest way to access tickets to your events. For more information visit Ticketmaster.co.nz/mobileticket

Join us for a behind-the-scenes tour of Wellington's iconic St James Theatre and The Opera House. Discover hidden stories, architectural splendour, and rich theatrical history as you step behind the velvet curtain and explore two of the city's most beloved performance spaces. Led by expert guides, this tour takes you on a journey through over a century of theatre magic. Tread the boards in the footsteps of legendary performers. Uncover the secrets of stagecraft and marvel at the breathtaking details of these heritage-listed buildings. From opulent foyers and ornate auditoriums to backstage corridors brimming with history, you'll see the theatres as you've never seen them before.

events.id: Alpha Course 2026-St Mary's Anglican Church, 170 Karori Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Alpha Course 2026
events.venue: St Mary's Anglican Church, 170 Karori Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Curious about life, faith, meaning… or just keen for a good conversation over a great meal?
You’re invited to Alpha at Karori Anglican, starting Tuesday 22 April in Te Kororia.
Alpha is a relaxed, friendly space where you can explore the big questions of life and the Christian faith—no pressure, no prior knowledge needed, and absolutely no question is off limits. Each evening includes a shared meal, a short video, and open discussion where you can share as much or as little as you like.
Best of all? It’s completely free, dinner is provided, and you’re welcome to just come along to the first session and see if it’s for you—no obligation to continue. If you are keen to continue it runs for around 10 weeks.
Bring a friend, or come on your own—you’ll be warmly welcomed either way.
We’d love to see you there!
Please contact office@karorianglican with any questions or to let us know you are coming.

events.id: Women's Self Defence - Free Intro Night-293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Women's Self Defence - Free Intro Night
events.venue: 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We believe every woman should be able to move and live freely, without fear of harassment or assault. Krav Maga has proven to be highly empowering for women all around the world. We offer this course to women to learn to deal with women-related challenges, encouraging every woman to overcome real threats, anxiety or fear, with confidence, skills, strength.
In an ideal world, women would not need to learn to protect themselves. However, until some deeply ingrained societal problems are resolved, it is smart and necessary to have some skills to be able to look after yourself and loved ones with. In the meantime, we encourage every women to approach this training with personal development, personal growth, enjoyment and empowerment being the motivating purpose.
We understand women often prefer to train in a women's class. This is our Women's (13+) class called ROAR.
Beginning in 2018, this program was created to provide a safe, holistic and supportive class where women can experience empowerment through training. You will learn to find your voice, inner strength and resolve, while learning physical skills such as how to escape and overpower an assailant, counter grabs, abduction attempts, chokes, hair grabs, pushing and shoving, being hit, being pinned to the ground and sexual assault attempts. We will help you unleash your protective feminine fighting spirit.
The Intro Night is the ideal time for women interested in the Women's Class to try a class first, see how we train, who we are, and gain some valuable basic skills, before deciding to join the full course. (Note: Full course is 11 weeks long). No cost to attend the initial Intro Night.
Please email contact@tacticalsurvival.co.nz to enquire or book your spot.
More Info: www.tacticalsurvival.co.nz/womens
After Intro, classes run every Wednesday night 7:00pm - 8:00pm (Thistle Hall Cuba St)

events.id: Jason Leong: In Laughing Memory-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Jason Leong: In Laughing Memory
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
DOORS OPEN 6:30pm
SHOW START 7:00pm

After losing one of his best friends to cancer, Dr Jason Leong did a very Dr Jason Leong thing to process his grief: write a comedy show about it. 
Join him in his most personal hour yet, as he navigates loss and love with laughter. 
Dr Jason Leong is a medical doctor turned comedian and he never fails to bring that up, unprompted. He has had two Netflix specials, performed at Just For Laughs Montreal in 2022, and won the 7th Annual International Hong Kong Comedy Competition. He tours around the world because he loves comedy and ultimately, money.

events.id: polymer clay workshop - jules + the wildflowers craft club-7A Rintoul St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: polymer clay workshop - jules + the wildflowers craft club
events.venue: 7A Rintoul St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
polymer clay workshop: slow down. make something.
Join me for a fun, beginner-friendly polymer clay workshop designed for adults who want to learn something new and enjoy a creative evening together.
In this 2.5 hour session, we’ll play with colour combinations, I will show you a simple technique to make your own earrings or small accessories.
I’ll guide you step-by-step as you create your slab and cut your pieces, with plenty of time to experiment and enjoy the process. Think relaxed vibes, and the simple joy of making something with your hands.
You’ll head home with some seriously cute, handmade pieces!
I will bake your creations after 1.5h of making and you can either sit with a tea/coffee and snacks for some chats or keep playing with polymer clay's million possibilities. Then we'll assemble your pieces to take home.


April 22nd: abstract canes
June 26th: floral slab

Soft drinks and snacks will also be available to keep our creative energy flowing.
No experience is needed — just come along and make something cute!

events.name: Karli Tyla - Free Live Music Wednesdays
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Lift your midweek with Moon's Free Live Music Wednesdays, your weekly escape into the sonic stratosphere!
Every week, we feature a rotating lineup of incredible musicians - this week we have Karli Tyla!
Karli is an indie-pop singer-song writer from Ngāti Whātua ki Ōrākei. Karli recently opened at Party4t, a fundraising event for PATHA. When she is not maintaining a below average gpa as a law student she is spending as much time as possible in te taiao. Karli writes about her experiences as a takatāpui wahine Māori; ranging from situationship heartbreaks to studying at the heart of empire and everything in between.
Come by early for our $15 margherita pizza specials, served hot 4-9pm.
Perfect for date night, catch-ups, or just floating solo through the sound waves. Wednesdays at Moon are the place to orbit!

events.id: Stargazing 201-40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Stargazing 201
events.venue: 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you ever wanted to look a little deeper into the night sky, not just to identify what you’re seeing, but to understand how it all works? In this brand new two‑hour live planetarium session, we’ll explore the stories, science, and structures that shape our view of the cosmos.
Stargazing 201 builds on the foundations of our beginner programme, taking you further into seasonal constellations, cultural astronomy, the lives and evolution of stars, and the possibility of life beyond Earth. You’ll also journey outward through the observable universe and discover how telescopes allow us to see far beyond the limits of our eyes.
Sit back and enjoy an immersive guided tour of the universe under our planetarium dome, brought to life with our high‑definition laser projectors. Designed for curious minds ready to go further, Stargazing 201 is ideal for those who’ve attended Stargazing 101 but remains accessible and engaging for all.
Includes:
- Intermission with refreshments
- Access to Space Place’s galleries and interactive exhibits
20% discount at Space Place Shop (excludes food and already discounted items)
Doors open 6:30pm | Event 7:00pm-9:00pm | Space Place | R16

Handsome Tours are proud to present Sleaford Mods 2026 Australian & New Zealand Tour
This will be Sleaford Mods first time back to Australian and New Zealand soil since May and June of 2023, where they performed at events like Sydney's Vivid LIVE and Hobart's Dark Mofo.

events.id: A Place to Sit – Opening-26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: A Place to Sit – Opening
events.venue: 26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
As days pass us by and the seasons change, it is a gift to be able to crop into the details of our daily landscapes. A kitchen table surrounded by family, or the bush as we traverse up into its hills - there is tangibility of time and much needed stillness to be found in both Aida Smith and Benjamin Cotter Dephoff’s canvases. Twentysix Gallery is pleased to present their latest works in ‘A Place to Sit’ - the two artists' depictions of small, everyday rituals grounding us - providing a texture and meaning in what might seem like ordinary moments.
The two oil painters work with introspection and the rhythm of slow living, documenting the subtle shifts across our day to day. While Ben’s paintings are attuned to how time and weather shape his landscapes, Aida’s scenes from home seem suspended in time, with an ever so subtle mirroring of the changes in light, warmth and routine that mark our passage across the changing seasons. A tonal warmth spreading across them both as we enter autumn - reflective, inward and a time for slowing down.
Ben bases his practice in the bush, an ‘on the ground’ report of Aotearoa’s wild trails and the steps taken on them. A deep breath looking down from the top of a summit, as well as floral close ups from on the way up. His works are deep in tone, with the dark greens and brown of the landscape and wood walls in the firelight of a DOC hut, as a base for each scene he depicts in his perfectly detailed, almost photorealistic style.
Aida’s lens is closer up. The scene while sitting down with a cup of tea and a loved one or a candle lit in the evening - her people seem to have just left the frame, their presence felt at the edges. A looser stroke, a wider shadow - warming light that emanates from her canvases. Both Ben and Aida’s works are underscored with a recognition of how important it is to recharge - to leave the weekday rush for a while and take time in solitude, or else surrounded by your community, whether it is in the wild winds of the outdoors, or warm inside - intentional stillness, reflection and appreciation is nourishment for an active mind.
The exhibition runs from 24 April – 16 May 2026, with an opening celebration on Thursday, 23 April at 5pm.

events.id: Shadow Signals by Dan Beban and Tim Barlow-69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Shadow Signals by Dan Beban and Tim Barlow
events.venue: 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Daniel Beban and Tim Barlow's immersive, interactive sound installation - Shadow Signals, fills the environment of Toi Pōneke gallery for the final exhibition at the Abel Smith Street space.
Visitors activate the installation using their mobile phone’s inbuilt torch-lights to create sound. Movement of the phones through a variety of sculptural objects casts shadows onto solar panels which convert the optical signals into sonic information. The result is in an immediate and direct transposition of the optical into the audible, making possible a kind of ‘signal music’: electronic glitch-scapes, data-noise and shadow-tones.
Join us as we welcome them in to the Toi Pōneke whare!!

Anna’s dancing could be best described as a balanced blend of sensual bachata technique with flow, musicality, and softness.

events.id: Double Book Launch: Dinah Hawken and James Brown-57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Double Book Launch: Dinah Hawken and James Brown
events.venue: 57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us to launch two incisive new poetry collections - Peace & Quiet by Dinah Hawken and New Days for Old by James Brown.
New Days for Old is a life story in small scenes. Funny, moving, erotic, political and undeniably odd, it turns our most familiar-feeling days into something altogether new.
Each scene in this book is like a 1-minute pop song: its depths are at first easy to miss. But as the story proceeds, the grand scheme of things hoves into view: we are born, we crawl and then are carried away, and everything is a-shimmer, even the disappointments.
Dinah Hawken's Peace & Quiet queries the use of poetry in times of ecological and political turbulence, invoking both human voices and the voices — ‘the silt and the slash’ — of the natural world.
Powerful and illuminating, these poems show that peace, gentleness and reflection are a form of resistance.

The Undercurrent presents the Wellington Calling Open Mic!
The Undercurrent is now hosting the Wellington Calling Open Mic and we're looking for diverse acts to contribute! Each performer gets the chance to have a six-minute set with a microphone, guitar and piano provided. This is an originals only event. We want to encourage new creative voices to be heard. “We want to provide a positive and encouraging environment for established and fledgling acts to shine in. It's a pretty inspiring place to perform,” says host Steven Hood.
Each performer will need to sign up in person before 7pm and registrations of interest need to be emailed to openwandw@gmail.com before April 23.
The feature act is Noah Bryn, a singer songwriter, composer, guitarist and producer from Wellington, who makes music drawing influences from folk, jazz, rock, funk, blues and soul. Koha entry

events.id: City Nature Challenge 2026-15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: City Nature Challenge 2026
events.venue: 15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington is joining the world in the global wildlife count! Will you join us? All you need to do is
explore,
snap, and
share.
A bunch of local events scattered throughout Te Upoko o te Ika (Wellington Region) will help you do just that! Throughout 24-27 April, we are teaming up with other nature enthusiasts and experts to bring local City Nature Challenge-inspired events to you. Updates on community events near you (and some spot prizes!!!) will be posted to this event page.
Annually, the City Nature Challenge invites cities from across the world to compete to see who can log the most nature observations on iNaturalist over four days.
Not only is this challenge a fun way to explore nature in our own backyard, but knowing what species are in our city and where they are helps us study and protect them.
Taking part is simple:
explore wildlife Friday 24 to Monday 27 April
take a photo of what you find
share it to iNaturalist app or website
For more information on the City Nature Challenge, visit our website

events.name: Rova tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Cream Events
THIS IS THE HOO. ROVA is bringing it home.
After shutting down dancefloors across the UK and Europe, Aotearoa producer & DJ ROVA returns home for THIS IS THE HOO, a full scale NZ tour hitting Dunedin, Wellington, Auckland and Christchurch this April.
Since discovering his passion for music production in 2019, Rova has quickly become one of the fastest growing names in drum and bass. Each release showcases his distinct flair for pushing bass music boundaries, with his sound now turning heads around the globe.
With every show, he creates an electrifying atmosphere, blending new sounds with his signature style. His ability to connect with fans through his hard hitting live sets has led to sell out shows across Europe, Australia and New Zealand, this tour marks a full circle moment.
THIS IS THE HOO isn’t just a tour. It’s a homecoming, a statement, and a reminder that Rova is firmly in his moment.

events.id: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies-29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies
events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne and the Wellington Astronomical Society have teamed up to bring you an evening of ‘Dark Skies’.
Astronomer Holly McClelland will begin the evening with a talk on the importance of dark skies, both to the natural ecosystem and to human health.
Illustrated with stunning image from the Cretney Observatory near Martinborough, Holly will introduce us to the night sky, so the next time you are looking to the stars you can not only find the Southern Cross, but also the Galactic Kiwi.
Weather permitting, we will then have an hour of telescope viewing from the Zealandia carpark. Alternatively, if the weather isn’t ideal, you’ll get the chance to get an up-close look at some of the incredible astronomical equipment used by the Wellington Astronomical Society. WAS members will be available to answer your questions.
About Holly:
Holly is an astronomer and science communicator. She has a Bachelor of Science in Astronomy from the University of Canterbury, was a senior astronomy guide in Tekapo, leading groups on once-in-a-lifetime stargazing experiences. Holly is also on the council of the Wellington Astronomy Society, helping to make events like this possible.
Please note: If the weather is clear for star gazing, the Visitor Centre carpark will be closed off for this activity. Parking is still available at our overflow carparking on Birdwood Street, but we encourage guests to consider public transport, taxi and rideshare options.
Doors open at 5:30pm with complimentary mocktails, talk begins at 6pm.

events.id: ANZAC Day - 2026 Open Day-66 Wrights Hill Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: ANZAC Day - 2026 Open Day
events.venue: 66 Wrights Hill Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
NEWS RELEASE :
FOR IMMEDIATE USE - April 2026
ANZAC DAY - OPEN DAY- SATURDAY 25th April: 10AM - 4PM.
Wellington’s historic Wrights Hill Fortress in Karori is open for public inspection again on ANZAC Day, SATURDAY, April 25th. The World War Two coastal battery first opened to the public in 1989 and will be accessible between 10am - 4pm.
On ANZAC Day people are invited to self-guide themselves through the tunnels and gun emplacements. The walk usually takes about one hour. Bring a torch with you for some fun!
Visitors will be able to explore the 620 metres of underground tunnels with a free map and history pamphlet. They will see the engine room, shell stores, plotting rooms and the huge gun pit where Gun Number 1 used to stand, as well as a replica of the 10-metre-long gun barrel.
Also on show will be historic photos, and a DVD of how a 9.2-inch gun operates will be showing as well. The World War Two Fortress will also have period themed displays. It’s only open to the public on four Open Days a year.
Wrights Hill Fortress was built from 1942 to protect Wellington from Japanese bombardment. In the early 1960’s, the guns were cut up for scrap and sold, ironically, to the Japanese.
Admission is $20 for a family of two adults and three children under 15, or $10 per adult and $5 per child. (Sorry no EFTPOS -CASH ONLY) Access is via Karori Road, Campbell Street, and Wrights Hill Road. All proceeds go to the continuing restoration of the Fortress. There is limited car parking at the summit of Wrights Hill. Please Park sensibly and use walking tracks.
Visitors to the Fortress in Wrights Hill Reserve can also enjoy picnic sites and panoramic views from the top of the hill. There will be a BBQ sausage sizzle and soft drinks on sale. For safety reasons no jandals please.

Also on show will be historic photos, and a DVD of how a 9.2-inch gun operates will be showing as well.

events.id: Middle Age Spread-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Middle Age Spread
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We start our celebrations with ‘Middle Age Spread’ by Sir Roger Hall directed by Susan Wilson. The 25th April has been purposely selected because Circa’s official opening was the 23rd April 1976 which was picked as it is the same as Shakespeare’s birthday. We will be celebrating after the play reading with birthday cakes and drinks, as part of the performance.
First performed at the original Circa Theatre in Harris Street in 1977, directed by Michael Haigh, starring Dorothy McKegg, Grant Tilly, Jane Waddell, Ray Henwood, Donna Akersten and John Reid. Three couples attend a dinner none of them wants to be at.
Middle Age Spread ran for 15 months in London’s West End, winning the Lawrence Olivier Award for Comedy of the Year. In 1978 it became a successful NZ film directed by John Reid. This brilliant and poignant tragicomedy still remains a very significantly relevant social commentary on New Zealand Society . . . what’s changed in Aotearoa in 50 years, and then again… What hasn’t?

events.id: Faultline Ultra--Wellington NZ
events.name: Faultline Ultra
events.long_description:
Calling all athletes on foot or bike. National and international competitors are invited to test their endurance at Faultline Ultra. For runners and mountain bikers, it’s a dream come true — the ultimate stamina challenge set against the wildly beautiful Wellington landscape. 
Race options
In April 2026, the hills and trails around Wellington offer six possible routes for athletes to push their limits. Choose from the following lengths depending on your ability and appetite.
6km – Magnitude 6.0
12km – The Tremor
25km – After Shock
53km Ultra – Shake and Bake
103km Ultra – The Worlds End
100 Miler – Insanity
The fault lines around Wellington have pushed and pulled the land to create soaring peaks, rugged ridgelines, and bush-clad hills. Athletes can choose trails that wind through Wellington City, Karori, Porirua, Belmont, the Hutt River Trail, and Kāpiti. Sign up and compete — you’re likely to get the best after-match stories you will ever tell.

events.id: Hidde Van Wee tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Hidde Van Wee tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Lacuna
Lacuna presents Dutch selector Hidde Van Wee live at Meow Nui.
One of the most exciting emerging names out of Europe’s underground, Hidde Van Wee lands in Wellington fresh off his latest Eastenderz release ‘Shamans Vision’. With a sound rooted in minimal house and rolling techno, his sets are driven by deep grooves, crisp percussion, and an instinct for building energy across the dancefloor.
Whether in the booth or in the studio, Hidde delivers a sound that’s tight, hypnotic, and built for late nights - the kind that lock you in and don’t let go.
Supporting on the night is Lacuna resident Jack Ritchie, bringing his signature selection, alongside Auckland-based duo Cash - a high-energy pairing making serious waves with their productions and club-ready sound.
Expect a full night of forward-thinking underground music, dialled-in energy, and a packed dancefloor from start to finish.

events.id: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a global phenomenon hailed as the most electrifying celebration of the King of Pop’s music. ‘The King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience’ makes its way to New Zealand — and Wellington — for the first time. If you never had the chance to see Michael Jackson live, this is the closest you’ll ever get. From the famous moonwalk to the spine-tingling vocals, every moment of this production channels the spirit of the King of Pop at his peak. Witness the power of hits like Billie Jean, Thriller, Beat It, Smooth Criminal, and plenty more. 
The star of the show is TJ Cappola. His stunning vocals and commanding stage presence make for a flawless embodiment of Michael Jackson. With sold-out performances across Australia and Asia, thousands have been mesmerised by his performance. Backing TJ is a live band and world-class dancers. The replica costumes and cutting-edge lighting and visual display make this more than just a show — it’s an experience.

events.id: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make your ANZAC long weekend a winner by cheering on the One New Zealand Warriors at Hnry Stadium. Aotearoa’s favourite NRL team is heading south to give fans outside of Auckland the opportunity to see them play. The Hnry Stadium event is only the fourth time the team has staged the prestigious ANZAC Day game in Aotearoa. It’s going to be a rare chance to see them in action. Australian team the Dolphins come from Redcliffe in Queensland.  They are sure to bring the energy needed to make this an epic battle. One New Zealand Warriors have had a sensational season and are currently in fourth place on the NRL Premiership leaderboard. 
From Hnry Stadium all the way along the waterfront, Wellington will be buzzing for the long weekend. Head on down for top-tier footy, food, and entertainment. It’s a great way to celebrate the spirit of the ANZACs as two countries united in history reignite their trans-Tasman rivalry. Be there when the capital goes Warrior mode.

events.name: Definitely Maybe
events.venue: 21 Blair St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
DEFINITELY MAYBE – MANCHESTER MEETS DUBLIN
Ah here… this one’s gonna be a belter.
We’re mashin’ up the swagger of Manchester with a proper Dublin knees-up, Britpop anthems meets Irish classics, all night long.
Think Definitely Maybe, Blur vs Oasis carry-on… then into the good stuff from Thin Lizzy, U2, The Cranberries, Ash and plenty more besides.
It’s big choruses, arms round shoulders, and a room full of legends thinkin’ they’re Liam Gallagher after a few creamy ones.
Pints will be flowin’. Tunes will be flyin’. Not a dry throat or quiet corner in the gaff.
So go on… get in early, grab a pint, and don’t be ridin’ off before the last tune.

events.id: Natural Textured Rings-65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Natural Textured Rings
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special!
Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!
Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!
Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.
This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.
Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.
Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
Workshop fee is $165, Early bird pricing $148 (ends 30 March).

Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

events.id: Awaken the Witch Within-18-32 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Awaken the Witch Within
events.venue: 18-32 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Awaken the Witch Within journey for the Samhain energy we have coming up on the 30th April.
Guided Meditation - Shadow Work - Dark Goddess - Feminine Empowerment.
Karmic Ties, Soul Contracts - these we can revoke during this process.
Connection and Reflection at this time when the veil between worlds is at its thinnest, with this Samhain energy we move through the veils, guided by Hecate, Baba Yaga, and Circe, to face the depths of the witch wound and the karmic threads that have bound us for lifetimes... this is nothing short of alchemy.
This gathering is a weaving of release and remembrance, a sacred space where grief, shame, and exile gave way to empowerment, clarity, and reclamation.
Together, we will be guided by Hecate through her labyrinth, step into the depths of Baba Yagas deep forest, and only emerge with Circe more whole, more aligned, and more fiercely connected to the truth of our power.
Are you ready to Awaken the Witch Within?
Are you ready to dive deep into your internal worlds and extract the most powerful version of self.
This is an inquiry and exploration of layers of self, with journaling prompts and expansion points before we go into the deep guided journey of alchemy.

events.id: Mushrooms and other fungi - guided walk--41.267300, 174.759000-Facebook
events.name: Mushrooms and other fungi - guided walk
events.long_description:
A walk through the gardens and bush at Ōtari Wilton’s Bush to explore and discover the ever-changing array of mushrooms and fungi that are an essential part of its biodiversity.
Meet at Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. It is easy walking and family groups are welcome. We ask that dogs stay at home for everyones comfort and a smooth tour experience.
This is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual guided walks programme. This year, they are on the fourth Sunday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush. Join the Trust for only $20 for an individual or $30 for a double/family for a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits. www.owbt.nz/memberships-legacies/
Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.id: Rope Social-293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Rope Social
events.venue: 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Cost: $10pp
What to expect:
Rope social is all about making rope bondage fun and accessible. It is a monthly get together for all people interested in rope bondage and is open to all skills levels. Whether you have rope running through your veins or you wouldn't recognise a knot if it were tied on you, feel free to come and hang out (this could be taken very literally if you're keen!) No worries if you don't have your own rope, there's rope available for you to use. Rope social is a bit like a munch but with rope added. There'll be music to set the mood and plenty of good company so bring your excited pants! Since Wellington is known for rope in NZ there will always be attendees who can tell you all about the fun you can have with rope and even show a thing or two. Come along and share your knowledge and experiences, introduce a new person to rope, learn new tricks, get to know some like-minded people and have some fun
As with any kind of rope bondage safety is paramount - both physical and emotional. It is a requirement of everyone (both riggers and rope bottoms) attending Rope Social that you familiarise yourself with the safety information in these two videos: [Rope bondage safety part 1][https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lr4EtgSS3qM] [Rope bondage safety part 2][https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9VBBdCWLcPw]
Event Code Of Conduct *R18+ *No unsafe bondage. *Practice only consensual bondage. *No touching other people or their rope without their express permission. *Riggers must have safety shears or rope cutters on them at all times. *Please only engage in rope activities here, no other type of play. *Please tidy up after yourself. We have climbing tape available to use, but please remember to put it back when you're done. *Photography is permitted only with explicit consent from all of those in the image, including those in the background. Photography is only permitted to allow people to document their own work. No photographers are allowed at the event. *We reserve the right to refuse admission.

events.id: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)-57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)
events.venue: 57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
No pressure, no plan—just new friends

Whether you’re visiting Wellington for the first time, recently relocated, or simply love connecting with curious people, this weekly meetup is the perfect way to share stories, make friends, and go explore, as a group, the magic that Wellington has to offer

What to expect?
• Casual & hostless: no formal host, no schedule: just show up, be yourself, meet fellow solo travellers & expats and decide as a group where you feel like going
• Central meeting point: it is surrounded by cultural landmarks, cafes, and restaurants, making it a convenient and safe starting point.
• Open to everyone: whether you are a local, an expat or just passing through for a few days, you are welcome to join. (English is the primary language, but feel free to switch of course)

How to talk with strangers?
(and create meaningful connections)
To help you get started, I curated a little game of ridiculously good questions you can ask to break the ice, shift the conversation from dull to intriguing and fun, and eventually create meaningful connection quickly. I hope this helps!
www.pierrebonvalot.com/good-questions

The only things you need to do are…
• Leave a message in the comment section to let the others know that you will actually be there (I don’t want you to end up alone there

• If you can’t make it anymore, no problem! But please update your RSVP to let the others know
• Please be gentle and patient with each other. We all want to make good friends here.

Why join?
• Meet open-minded travellers and locals who are keen to connect, learn and have fun together.
• Experience Wellington... as a group.
• Be part of a global community that celebrates solo travelling and meaningful connections between foreign and locals.

How does it works?
1. Arrive at the meeting spot at the scheduled time.
2. Look for other joiners—friendly faces and curious explorers.
3. Introduce yourself, connect with others, and decide as a group where to go.

When & where?
• Day: every Sunday
• Time: 4:00 PM (meetup sometimes displays a different time zone: but it’s always 4pm local time)
• Location: in front of the Albatross Fountain
Hostless: I am setting up the event for you all to meet, but I won't be there myself this time

Hi my name is Pierre
I created this Meetup group because I want to see more human connection happen in the real world. I feel many of us want this, but don't exactly know how to go about it. I hope this helps a little bit.
I also work as a coach: I help people who feel stuck in their life to reconnect with themselves, create real change and ultimately live more fulfilling lives.

events.id: Songwriters' Open Mic at Moon-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Songwriters' Open Mic at Moon
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Songwriters’ Open Mic at Moon
The first of the 2026 series of Songwriters’ Open Mic sessions at Moon will be on Sunday April 19 kicking off shortly after the bar opens at 4pm. There will be sessions each month through until November, on the afternoons preceding the Songwriters' Showcase concerts scheduled for the evenings.
These sessions are solely for folk to perform their own original songs. So what’s the drill? Turn up, make yourself known to whoever appears to be in charge, and get your name on the list. Performances will then be in the order called by the organisers - no pre-booking of times, or reserving spots. Sets will be either 2 or 3 songs depending on the level of demand.
So regardless whether you are a “newby” taking your first steps beyond your bedroom, or an “old hand” wanting to test drive new material, or just get some “open wicket practice”, this is the open mic for you. If you fancy getting to play one of the Showcase concerts this is the right place to audition. And it's not just a chance to perform but to meet and get to know other songwriters around town.
Performers at the open mic get entry to the following Songwriters show for $10 (discounted from the regular punters price of $15.
The Poneke Songwriters Collective thanks Moon Bar for supporting these events and continuing to support the local music community
As the cool kids say “be there or be quadrilateral”.
Pic - Random “guy with a guitar”

events.id: The Weed Eaters | Preview Screening-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Weed Eaters | Preview Screening
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
4.20 pm preview screening of THE WEED EATERS!
Grab your best buds, swing by the concession stand for some munchies and be some of the first in Wellington to watch this hilarious stoner movie with a twist.
When Jules joins her new boyfriend on a New Year’s Eve getaway with his old high school mates, she’s looking to get some peace and quiet, smoke a bit of weed, and make some new friends. But when the holidaymakers stumble across their host’s long-forgotten stash of ancient weed, things quickly take a turn for the nightmarish.

events.id: Ol' King Coleslaw...-18 Garrett St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Ol' King Coleslaw...
events.venue: 18 Garrett St, Wellington
events.long_description:
SUNDAY JAZZ @ THE ROGUE!
OL’ KING COLE brings you the hits and celebrates the music of the great Nat ‘King’ Cole and more! The group was originally inspired specifically with a focus on Nat’s early drum-less trio, which has now expanded for this special show.
Featuring Wellington based vocalist Wallace and prolific drummer Mike Jensen, the join the core trio of Louis Thompson-Munn (piano/vocals), Robert Henderson (double bass) & Lockie Bennett (guitar).
A great and lively performance is always a guarantee with the spirit of the old house band alive, before TVs in bars and when people would dance the night away!
Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY + KOHA encouraged.

events.id: Girl Next Door: the next chapter tour - Wellington-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Girl Next Door: the next chapter tour - Wellington
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jenni Smith is hitting the road this autumn for her “Girl Next Door: The Next Chapter” Tour - an intimate run of shows celebrating the release of her long-awaited debut album, The Girl Next Door (out March 27).
Fresh from nationwide chart success and years of diary-style storytelling, Jenni invites you into the next chapter of her world - one filled with red lipstick confidence, hopeless romantic daydreams, and the kind of songs that feel like pages torn straight from her journal. Expect heartfelt ballads, kitchen-dance anthems, and the stories behind the songs that built this era.
The tour kicks off in Auckland at Ponsonby Social Club on March 28th - a special album release party the night after the record drops - before heading to Christchurch’s Wunderbar on April 19th, and Wellington’s Moon on April 29th.
Joining Jenni for this run are three incredible artists:
Auckland - Blake Stainton
Christchurch - Steffany Beck
Wellington - Emma Kate
Whether you’ve been here since the first Taylor Swift cover or just found your way to The Girl Next Door, this tour is about celebrating the journey so far - and stepping into what comes next.
The next chapter starts here.

events.id: Rory Stuart Trio-Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Rory Stuart Trio
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Undercurrent presents the Rory Stuart Trio!
New York-based jazz guitarist and composer Rory Stuart is joined by two stalwarts of the Wellington jazz scene: Mark Lockett on drums and Elijah Mulheron on bass.
Rory Stuart is described by Jazz Times as “Perhaps THE most innovative straight-ahead jazz guitarist to emerge in years” and listed by Scott Yanow as one of the greatest living jazz guitarists. He has led critically acclaimed groups and his recordings have received 4 star reviews (Downbeat) and been listed in “10 Best of the Year” (Coda). He has also performed as a co-leader or sideperson with a truly diverse group of jazz masters, from Brother Jack McDuff and Bill Doggett to George Garzone, Billy Harper, Larry Coryell, and his former students Robert Glasper and Becca Stevens, to Steve Coleman’s early MBASE group with Geri Allen and Cassandra Wilson.
Sunday Apr 26
Doors 5pm
Music 5:30pm
$15
Tickets available online and on the door!
https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../Rory-Stuart-Trio.utr
Previous performance: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ycgoJzAGjKA&t=1150s
Another performance: https://youtu.be/JJTQnfKBGVQ?si=tPPFejHZiTmUN-PB

events.id: Book Launch: The Lost Climber’s Legacy by Julia Millen-57 Willis Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Book Launch: The Lost Climber’s Legacy by Julia Millen
events.venue: 57 Willis Street, Wellington
events.long_description: Come celebrate the launch of well known author Julia Millen's debut novel. Our guest speaker, Dr Kirsty Findlay, was a college classmate of Juliaʻs. Kirsty and Julia shared a passion for adventure fiction, notably Arthur Ransome. Kirsty became an academic; Julia a writer. In her study of Ransome’s work, Kirsty later discovered his ʻlost’ study of Robert Louis Stevenson. How appropriate to now be launching ‘The Lost Climber’s Legacy’.

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.
The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing
this album live is part of that.’
Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.
‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’

The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’ Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps.
In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.

events.id: Hudson Freeman-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hudson Freeman
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hudson Freeman is a Brooklyn-based Lofi-folk artist, inspired and forged by the DIY midwest. The 27-year-old Freeman continues to record and perform songs equal-parts resonant, reflective, and poignant. He has quickly emerged as one of indie's most enthralling new voices. His recent full-length, is a Folk Artist, helped solidify his presence as the kind of songwriter unafraid to tackle life's most draining contradictions: modern identity, digital disconnection, and faith.
Born to Evangelical missionaries, Hudson started writing songs upon a radical break at the age of thirteen when his family suddenly moved from the suburbs of Dallas to The Kingdom of Eswatini. The profound influence of indie big-hitters like Sufjan Stevens and Bon Iver as well as college years spent in Springfield, Missouri made a do-it-yourself midwesterner out of Hudson. Now, his new song "If You Know Me" has recently started drawing in attention from the masses, catapulting Freeman into a wider spotlight than ever. It's a moody blend of bedroom pop, folk, slowcore, and post-emo, stitched together by a singular guitar riff and mantra-style lyrics, confirming Hudson's undeniable knack for melody.

events.id: RADAR ANGEL | BEET-WIX | MARCH OF THE KORORĀ-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: RADAR ANGEL | BEET-WIX | MARCH OF THE KORORĀ
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Radar Angel are a four-piece synthfoolery ensemble who blend industrial grit with lush nostalgic sounds from the dawn of the digital age, all with a lavish theatricality and pop sensibility that is guaranteed to get your limbs thrashing.
beet-wix is the solo electronic project of NZ Nintendo-DS-wielding weirdo Lucky Pollock, where they craft noisy-pop ballads on a Nintendo DS originally borrowed from the Ōtepoti art school (where they were not a student).
March of the Kororā are a pair of multi-instrumentalists trying to make sense of ecological collapse, injustice and resistance. Born out of jazz jams, space explorations and rage.
Doors 7PM, music from 7.30PM.
$10 pre-sales (UTR), $15 door sales.

events.id: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to Australia in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits. Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail. Tickets on sale Thursday 26th February 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go. Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone". The album's success propelled Saosin into mainstream recognition, earning them a dedicated global fanbase and cementing their status as one of the most influential bands of their era.
Saosin were first noticed by New Zealand fans at the Taste of Chaos tour at St James in 2006 alongside acts Taking Back Sunday, Underoath, Anti-Flag, Thursday and Senses Fail. They continued to come back to these shores in 2010 with Bless the Fall for one Auckland show
Now, 20 years on they’ll perform their self-titled album in full across New Zealand alongside their biggest hits next April.
Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years. With Reber back at the helm, Saosin is redefining their future. The chemistry among the original lineup has sparked creative momentum, leading to new material, revitalized performances, and expanded vision. The band is once again a driving force in post-hardcore, proving that while time may pass, authenticity and connection endure. This chapter in Saosin’s journey is both a homecoming and a launching point for what’s next.

Supporting Saosin on this special run is Senses Fail, These guys are also no strangers to New Zealand having been here for the Taste of Chaos tour in 2006.. The band have been called any number of things across their near 25 years as a band – emo, screamo, post-hardcore, metalcore, pop punk. It’s testament to the band’s perpetual evolution as much as it is their steely endurance – a quality which frontman Buddy Nielsen has required bottomless reserves of along the ride. It’s through the songs of Senses Fail that Nielsen has forever made sense of and peace with life’s highs and lows; a journey that began in the leafy suburbs of Ridgewood, New Jersey with the iconic Gold-selling debut album Let It Enfold You, and which has since seen Senses Fail become one of rock music’s most cherished, celebrated names.

Bringing together two giants of their genre, don't miss Saosin and Senses Fail do what they do best - tickets available Thursday 26th February 2026 at 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Tickets on sale.
Saosin 2026 Tour Dates
Sat 18th Apr - Studio The Venue (Auckland)
Sun 19th April - San Fran (Wellington)
Mon 20th April Sydenham Underpass (Christchurch)

events.venue: St Andrews Church & Hall, 28 The Terrace, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Artists
Tom Rainey, Conductor / Piano
Jimmy Rainey, Saxophone
Thomas Eves, Trumpet
Cameron Pearce, Composer / Trumpet
Barrett Hocking, Trumpet
Scott Taitoko, Trombone
Pablo Ruiz Henao, Low Brass
Joe McCallum, Drums
Symposium Brass members have performed extensively with all of New Zealand’s professional orchestras and with artists and bands ranging from James Morrison and Diana Krall to The Black Seeds and Salmonella Dub.
Their programme will bring together the best of the orchestral and jazz worlds, including the world premiere of "Progression" by Symposium Brass trumpeter and composer Cameron Pearce, which pulls colours from composers Aaron Copland, Steve Reich, and Gabriel Fauré along with trumpeters Kenny Wheeler and Ron Miles, and new material composed by Pearce for this tour.

events.id: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band-81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band
events.venue: 81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Does Irish music get your feet tapping? This is your opportunity to get up and dance to some cracking tunes at our evening Céilí on Saturday 18th of April.
Come to St Michael’s Hall in Kelburn to meet new people and enjoy a great time in a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. No dancing experience needed, just a bit of coordination, some energy, and a big sense of fun!
Experienced caller Helen Stonehouse will guide you through all the dances. Our amazing Round The House Céilí Band, featuring Roger Stonehouse, Em Griffiths, and Thomas Stonehouse, will be playing lively jigs and reels and polkas for us.
Handy information:
- Dancing starts at 7:30pm
- Age 10 upwards. Under 16s accompanied by an adult
- Bring a water bottle, hydration is key!
- Parking is limited, we recommend catching the bus. Routes 4, 18, and 21 all stop right next to the hall.
- If you’re unwell then please stay home this time, we’ll see you at the next one
Door sales may be available on the day (cash only): General Admission $30, Unwaged $20.
Pro tip - online tickets will be cheaper!
This event is part of our Set Dance day, and there will be workshops running during the day. Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance. Have a look here to see what it's all about: https://www.facebook.com/reel/1062173322541491.
If you’re interested, head over to Humanitix to get a full day package and fill your weekend with dancing!

events.long_description:
Directed by Rob Reiner
Starring Jerry O'Connell, Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, Kiefer Sutherland
40th Anniversary
--- After learning that a stranger has been accidentally killed near their rural homes, four Oregon boys decide to go see the body. On the way, Gordie Lachance (Wil Wheaton), Vern Tessio (Jerry O'Connell), Chris Chambers (River Phoenix) and Teddy Duchamp (Corey Feldman) encounter a mean junk man and a marsh full of leeches, as they also learn more about one another and their very different home lives. Just a lark at first, the boys' adventure evolves into a defining event in their lives. ---
Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.
At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!
Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.
The upstairs bar will open at 7:15pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 7:40pm and the feature begins at 7:50pm.
*No dietaries catered for.
Presented in 4K

events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Directed by Edward D. Wood Jr.
Starring Bela Lugosi, Criswell ., Tor Johnson, Vampira .
Residents of California's San Fernando Valley are under attack by flying saucers from outer space. The aliens, led by Eros (Dudley Manlove) and his assistant, Tanna (Joanna Lee), intend to conquer the planet by resurrecting corpses in a Hollywood cemetery.
The living dead -- a cape-wearing ghoul (Bela Lugosi), a vampire (Vampira) and a slow-footed cop (Tor Johnson) who was killed for his nosiness -- stalk curious humans who wander into the cemetery looking for evidence of the UFOs.

events.id: 1/3 Octave Band | Bridget Kelly & Peter Liley | Grant/Winterwood-Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: 1/3 Octave Band | Bridget Kelly & Peter Liley | Grant/Winterwood
events.venue: Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
1/3 Octave Band is the ambient/noise/drone project of Jules Desmond (Black Boned Angel) and Bill Wood (Birdcatcher) who conjure slow moving walls of eerie and epic electric guitar. Active since the late 90s, they've released a huge discography on labels like Celebrate PSI Phenomenon and Pseudo Arcana.
www.thirdoctaveband.bandcamp.com
--
The improvising duo of Bridget Kelly (reeds, harp) & Peter Liley (saxophone & electronics) will combine their unique creative voices and impeccable musicality moving from lyrical melodies to unearthly growls, creating meditative yet visceral compositions in real time.
---
Grant / Winterwood: Erika Grant and Zac Winterwood join forces for a performance utilising hammer dulcimer, shruti box, 12 stringed acoustic guitar, and other various instruments.
Erika Grant is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 30+ years musical experience in bands including Orchestra of Spheres, and Cookie Brooklyn and the Crumbs. Over the past few years Erika has developed a sound bathing practice using gongs, bowls and chimes. She is interested in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing.
Zac Winterwood (NZ/AUS) is a musical outlier whose art lives within the tradition/discipline of minimalism. Since the late 1990s, he has been a purveyor in the art of noise, ambient, drone, field recording and experimental music, whose work has been compared to Low, Terry Riley, Brian Eno and Neil Young.
https://www.rnz.co.nz/.../wash-the-mind-with-a-gong-bath
https://winterwood.bandcamp.com/
____________________________________________________

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Fri 1 May | Michael Fowler Centre-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Fri 1 May | Michael Fowler Centre
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.
The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’
Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.
‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’
