events.id: Public Service Broadcasting | Meow, Wellington-9 Edward Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Public Service Broadcasting | Meow, Wellington
events.venue: 9 Edward Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pre-sale starts Thu 05 Feb at 10am (local)
General tickets on sale Fri 06 Feb at 10am (local)
Sign up for pre-sale access
PRE-SALE: https://bit.ly/PSB26_ps
Handsome Tours presents the return of London-based sonic outfit Public Service Broadcasting who return to Australia and New Zealand in May, bringing their powerful, cinematic live show to audiences across the southern hemisphere.
Since emerging into existence in 2009, Public Service Broadcasting – led by instrumentalist-auteur J. Willgoose, Esq. – have forged a singular musical identity, transforming archival narratives into rousing, emotionally resonant works that blend post-rock, electronics and orchestral grandeur.
“We’ve been asking about coming back to Australia and New Zealand for what feels like an age, and we’re really pleased to be able to announce these shows at last. We hope as many people can come and join us as possible and look forward to seeing you all soon.” – Public Service Broadcasting
Renowned for their visually arresting shows and ability to turn historical narratives into shared, communal experiences, Public Service Broadcasting’s return to Australia and New Zealand promises an unforgettable live encounter – one that is at once awe-inspiring, moving and exhilarating.

events.id: ULCERATE + Vicissitude-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: ULCERATE + Vicissitude
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Via Debemur Morti:
“After triumphantly casting off the shackles of claustrophobic dissonance on 2020's lauded ‘Stare Into Death and Be Still’, New Zealand unorthodox Death Metal legends ULCERATE up the ante even further with mind-bending 7th album ‘Cutting the Throat of God’.
An acknowledgement that the band's most powerful and affecting material leans to the melodic side, this refined and hook-laden new record is a self-sufficient universe unto itself, a conflagration of inventively visceral Death/Black Metal where dizzying experimentation meets head-nodding abandon - obscenely twisted and addictive, heart-stopping in its depth of feeling.”
To complete the touring cycle for ‘Cutting…’, the band will undertake seven Australasian headlining dates in 2026. Special guests for the New Zealand leg are the utterly deadly Vicissitude.

events.id: The Room-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Room
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Room has been slaying audiences now for over 20 years throughout the world with rabid fans called Roomies screaming out lines and tossing plastic spoons in the air. Why? No matter what you’ve already heard, nothing can prepare you for the once-in-a-lifetime big-screen experience of seeing actor/director/producer Wiseau mumbling his way through this excruciating love triangle about a kind-hearted banker, his best friend and his cheating bride-to-be. It has many unforgettable scenes, but keep your eyes peeled for pictures of spoons, close football tossing, characters vanishing and emerging and even one that announces ‘I definitely have breast cancer’ as a throwaway conversation starter. Unmissable.
This is screening in Cinema 1 on DCP and is assigned seating. So organise with your fellow Roomies before buying tickets so you can sit together

Charting producer and established underground artist Efan is quickly rising in the UK rave scene, known for his electrifying DJ sets and unique production style. Efan has carved out a niche for himself by blending UK Garage, Bassline, Dubstep and Jungle influences into his music.
One of his standout collaborations is the track “Filthy Bassline” with UK legend Dizzee Rascal. He has collabs lined up with Tinie Tempah, Daniel Bedingfield, Tinchy Stryder, Badger, Champion and many more. Local support from Mason and Ansa

events.name: Sunday Jazz - Ol' King Cole
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring Ol' King Cole Quintet


Ol’ King Cole brings you the hits and celebrates the music of the great Nat ‘King’ Cole & performs the sounds of many other great tunes & artists of his era & more along the way. The group was originally inspired specifically with a focus on Nat’s early drumless trio which has now expanded for this special show and features Wellington based vocalist Wallace.

The core trio consisting of Robert Henderson on double bass along with Lockie Bennett on guitar and Louis Thompson-Munn on piano are now joined by prolific jazz drummer Mike Jensen.

A great and lively performance is always a guarantee with the spirit of the old house band alive, before TVs in bars and when people would dance the night away!

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.long_description:
Details
Directed by Rob Reiner
Starring Jerry O'Connell, Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, Kiefer Sutherland
40th Anniversary

--- After learning that a stranger has been accidentally killed near their rural homes, four Oregon boys decide to go see the body. On the way, Gordie Lachance (Wil Wheaton), Vern Tessio (Jerry O'Connell), Chris Chambers (River Phoenix) and Teddy Duchamp (Corey Feldman) encounter a mean junk man and a marsh full of leeches, as they also learn more about one another and their very different home lives. Just a lark at first, the boys' adventure evolves into a defining event in their lives. ---

Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.

At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!

Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.

The upstairs bar will open at 7:15pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 7:40pm and the feature begins at 7:50pm.

*No dietaries catered for.

Presented in 4K

events.id: Auditions - The Watsons by Laura Wade (Adapted from Jane Austen's unfinished novel)--41.296180, 174.773379-Facebook
events.name: Auditions - The Watsons by Laura Wade (Adapted from Jane Austen's unfinished novel)
events.long_description:
“The Watsons” by Laura Wade, based on the unfinished Jane Austen novel and directed by Amy Whiterod
What happens when the playwright loses the plot? And when the characters take charge of their story? What if the girl doesn’t want to fall in love with the rich, arrogant, entitled aristocrat? What happens then?
The Watsons was an unfinished novel of Jane Austen's. Laura Wade (“Home, I’m Darling”; television’s “Rivals”) picks up the story, makes it a play, and writes herself as a character to try to guide the story - but who could guess what Jane Austen wanted to happen?
A picture-perfect Austen story descends into theatrical chaos as the characters revolt against their playwright and the confines of the genre.
If you want to be part of telling a most unusual Austen story, please join us for auditions on Saturday 2nd of May and Sunday 3rd of May with callbacks the evening of Monday 4th of May.
Rehearsals start mid-May, and the season runs from 29th July - 8th August.
Booking an audition
If you wish to book an audition slot go to https://www.stagecraft.co.nz/auditions and complete the form.
Cast
We are looking for a large ensemble cast, and we will be open to all options for casting in terms of gender, ethnicity and ages for any of the roles. Please note some characters noted below will have some scenes which may include physical intimacy. There will also be some general physical demands for all actors in terms of dancing, battle scenes, and other physical activity (e.g. crawling or climbing over things), which we can adapt to actors’ abilities. A willingness to try things is what we are looking for!
Characters (acting ages)
Emma Watson (late teens - 20s) Youngest of three sisters, Emma begins the story as the picture-perfect Austen heroine, but she develops a rebellious streak and challenges the story her playwright has for her. One scene involving physical intimacy. Larger role.
Elizabeth Watson (20s-30s)
Eldest sister, she is sensible and dutiful. But even she has her limits. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy).
Margaret Watson (20s-30s)
Middle sister, Margaret is energetic, husband-obsessed and very silly. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy).
Robert Watson (30s-40s)
The older brother, a little patronising perhaps, but generally just curious about the world.
Mrs Robert Watson (30s-40s)
Robert’s wife Mary, very patronising and a little superior.
Mr Watson (60s+)
Father of the four Watson siblings, elderly and frail.
Nanny (30s+)
The Watsons’ servant, observant and amiable. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy).
Tom Musgrave (20s-40s)
A handsome gentleman and doesn’t he just know it.
Mr Howard (20s-40s)
Local clergyman, kind and thoughtful towards everyone. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy).
Charles Howard (10 years old - played by older actor)
Mr Howard’s nephew and ward, he is excited about life, loud and very curious about everything.
Lord Osborne (20s-40s)
Local aristocrat living at Osborne Castle, somewhat awkward and strange. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy).
Miss Osborne (20s-40s)
Sister to Lord Osborne, she is self-possessed and independent.
Lady Osborne (50+)
Mother of the Osborne siblings, very forthright and in charge of most situations. (May have scenes involving physical intimacy.)
Laura (30s-50s)
A fictional version of the playwright Laura Wade, who is drawn into the story she is telling. She is mostly sympathetic to her characters, slightly daunted at the task of adapting Jane Austen, and frequently overwhelmed when she is not in control of things. Larger role.
Ensemble (20s - 50s) including guests at the assembly, officers of the militia including Bertie, a dashing young officer who flirts with everyone (one scene involving physical intimacy), several smaller and non-speaking roles.
events.description:
The Watsons was an unfinished novel of Jane Austen's.
If you want to be part of telling a most unusual Austen story, please join us for auditions on Saturday 2nd of May and Sunday 3rd of May with callbacks the evening of Monday 4th of May.
Characters (acting ages) Emma Watson (late teens - 20s) Youngest of three sisters, Emma begins the story as the picture-perfect Austen heroine, but she develops a rebellious streak and challenges the story her playwright has for her.
Miss Osborne (20s-40s) Sister to Lord Osborne, she is self-possessed and independent.
She is mostly sympathetic to her characters, slightly daunted at the task of adapting Jane Austen, and frequently overwhelmed when she is not in control of things.

events.id: Te Tiriti ki Raukawa Moana tour — a guided He Tohu experience-Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Te Tiriti ki Raukawa Moana tour — a guided He Tohu experience
events.venue: Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join a guided He Tohu experience focusing on the Cook Strait Treaty sheet signed in Pōneke. Learn about Wellington and Kāpiti Coast signatories, view taonga connected to Te Tiriti o Waitangi, and take a hīkoi of Pipitea to key tohu whenua (landmarks).
Join a He Tohu tour to view the 132 signatures up close and to acknowledge the signing of Te Tiriti ki Raukawa Moana.
Please meet at our waka reception desk on the ground floor.
The Raukawa Moana sheet of te Tiriti o Waitangi
Also known as the Cook Strait sheet and the Henry Williams sheet, Te Tiriti ki Raukawa Moana is one of the nine sheets of te Tiriti o Waitangi. Together, they are an agreement between representatives of the British Crown and representatives of iwi and hapū Māori.
Who signed Te Tiriti ki Raukawa Moana?
On 2 April 1840, the missionary Henry Williams left Te Pēwhairangi Bay of Islands on the schooner Ariel to take te Tiriti o Waitangi around the motu. He arrived in Pōneke in mid-April, but for 10 days, he was unable to persuade rangatira to sign te Tiriti. Williams believed they were influenced by the activities of the New Zealand Company and its settlers, who had arrived here from September 1839.
Thirty-four rangatira eventually gave their agreement at a meeting with Williams on the Ariel on 29 April, including Te Rangitopeora (Ngāti Toa Rangatira, Ngāti Raukawa) — one of the 15 known rangatira wāhine (chiefly women leaders) who signed Te Tiriti.
Williams then continued his journey to Tōtara-nui (Queen Charlotte Sound), Kāpiti Island, Waikanae, Ōtaki, Tāwhirihoe pā at the mouth of the Rangitīkei River, the Manawatū, and Whanganui before sailing back to Waitangi with the sheet containing 132 signatures.
Viewing taonga from the collection
Taonga (treasures and unique items) relating to the Treaty of Waitangi will be on display for a special viewing.
Landmarks of Pipitea
Come on a hīkoi (walk) to see some tohu whenua (landmarks) of Pipitea and One Haukawakawa that tell the rich history of Mana Whenua, Te Āti Awa Taranaki Whānui ki Te Upoko o Te Ika, in Te Whanganui-a-Tara (Wellington).
Image: Learning facilitators Tanja Schubert-McArthur (left) and Tess Dalgety-Evans (foreground) taking a hīkoi (walking tour) to see tohu whenua (landmarks) of Pipitea.
events.description:
Learn about Wellington and Kāpiti Coast signatories, view taonga connected to Te Tiriti o Waitangi, and take a hīkoi of Pipitea to key tohu whenua (landmarks).

Please meet at our waka reception desk on the ground floor.

events.id: D&D In A Castle - 2nd May 2026-Hawkins Hill Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: D&D In A Castle - 2nd May 2026
events.venue: Hawkins Hill Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Dice, drama, and a whole lot of fantasy!
Come play in a castle!
Join us for some Dungeons & Dragons one shot adventures set inside a castle themed building up in the hills of Wellington. With stone walls, towers, chandeliers and pure fantasy vibes, this is the perfect setting to gather your party, roll initiative, and lose yourself in an afternoon of heroic storytelling.
Whether you’re a long-time adventurer or rolling dice for the first time, this is your chance to play D&D in a space that feels straight out of a fantasy story.
Event Details:
Date: Saturday 2nd of May 2026.
Time: 12.30pm arrival time to be shuttled to the venue. Be on time! We will shuttle you back to the pickup location afterwards.
Location: Wellington Castle. (*Arrive at the Brooklyn Windmill carpark for the shuttle service to the venue.)
Ages: 12+ (all kids playing must be accompanied by an adult who is playing also)
Experience Level: Beginner friendly (all levels welcome)
What’s Included
• Dice, miniatures and everything you need to play
• Pre generated characters provided (Please don’t bring your own – the DM’s have put a lot of thought into the sheets provided for their adventures.)
• A dedicated Dungeon Master and 6 Heroes per table.
• A custom-written adventure one-shot story by our professional dungeon masters
• Monster Miniatures, Battle maps and Terrain to explore during your game
• 3 hours of adventure with a break included for castle exploring
• Only Basic Snacks and Drinks available for purchase, no menu (this time… catch us next time!). Very welcome to bring your own snacks and drink bottles with you. No BYO alcoholic drinks or alcohol-free beer/wine etc.
Costumes: Optional but Awesome
Cloaks, armour, wizard hats, or your finest tavern attire. Costumes are encouraged but never required. Safe, non-functional props only, please. A small selection of fantasy vibes clothing will be onsite but being supplied by the team out of their own cupboards so limited in sizing etc. There for if the FOMO sets in.

events.id: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting in Central Park-Old Edwards Building, 131 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting in Central Park
events.venue: Old Edwards Building, 131 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join experts from Mountain's to Sea Wellington's Wai Connection team, and the Te Papa Natural History team to explore your local park by night, at this free City Nature Challenge spotlighting event at Central Park, Brooklyn.
With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi in your local park meet glowworm and ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. You'll also learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.
Outdoor event: This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
What is spotlighting? Ever played spotlight with a fish? Heaps of our native freshwater critters are nocturnal. Scientists often visit freshwater areas at nighttime with a torch, it’s the best time to see exactly who’s living in our awa!
What is Wai Connection? Wai Connection is a national programme delivered by Mountains to Sea Wellington aimed at connecting local communities to their waterways to improve them. We support communities and mana whenua with the knowledge, tools and expertise to monitor and care for their local freshwater places.

events.long_description:
Stitch & Butch @ Blondini’s!
A casual meet up of crafters within the rainbow community
New members welcome.
Bring your knitting/crochet/cross stitch/embroidery/crafting/art or just come and have a chat.
Feel free to come partway through the time or leave early as needed.
Stitch and butch is a playful twist on the stitch and b!tch name to reflect the queerness of the group. Attendees do not need to be in any particular subset of the queer community to be welcome.

Held on the 2nd and 4th Mondays of the month with an end of year break.

events.id: Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars
Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.
In a world designed to pull our attention in every direction, how do we train the mind to focus on what actually matters? This workshop blends practical focus strategies with insights from the Bhagavad-gita on understanding and directing the mind.
Join Sivananda for a relaxed, discussion-based session exploring tools to manage attention and deeper wisdom for those interested in lasting clarity and fulfillment.
Meet your host Sivananda:
Sivananda’s background is in both science and music, holding a Master's Degree in Data Science, a Bachelor of Science in Physics and a long history of playing and performing on the double bass. His passion for philosophy, music, good food and community attracted him to the bhakti lifestyle. Having studied and practiced the science of the ‘self’ for six years now, he thrives on drawing insights from contemporary science and combining it with yoga wisdom to encourage others to explore new perspectives.
6.15pm Tuesday 28th April
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.
Tickets available here~ bit.ly/eventTickets
Bhakti Lounge 1/175 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington. http://www.bhaktilounge.org.nz

events.venue: 56 Palmer Crescent, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
How prepared is Heretaunga for an emergency?
If you live or work in the Heretaunga/Wallaceville area, join us to get to know your Community Emergency Hub. Learn where to go for help during or after an emergency and discover how your local Community Emergency Hub supports the neighbourhood in a crisis.
Come along to St Brendan’s School Hall, Heretaunga to explore ways you can help your community, strengthen local connections, and build your emergency preparedness.
Everyone is welcome - and by attending, you’ll also have the chance to win a water tank to support your preparedness journey!
For more information, please email info@wremo.nz
We aim to make our community events inclusive to all people. If you are attending the event and have specific accessibility requirements, you can send us an email at info@wremo.nz with the event name and we will get in touch to see how we can meet your needs as best we can.

events.long_description:
The Canning Line Stand-Up Comedy Show returns to Double Vision Brewery, complete with a refresh!
After a successful run of sold-out shows over the last few years, The Canning Line will now be happening more often, moving into the recently revamped covered courtyard space, which is just the right mix of cosy and comfortable, and perfect for a relaxed night of laughs washed down with DVB's award-winning brews!
For each edition of the new Canning Line show, producer and MC Mike Fowler will present a different "batch" of local pro and up-and-coming comedians, all bringing their best gags, stories, and new jokes for your consideration. This first April edition has an extra special spark, with every single comedian all featuring in the upcoming NZ International Comedy Festival!
Introducing Batch 001 - April 29th, 7pm
Mike Fowler
Meaghan Fisher
Austin Harrison
Joana Joy
Keegan Thomas & Aaron James Douglas
Oliver Pol
Gabby Anderson
With a reduced ticket price of only $10 and a Happy Hour at the bar from 5pm, this will be an epic night of fun and a welcome escape from world events for a few hours. See you at the end of the line!

events.id: STEREOFACE - RADIOHEAD TRIBUTE-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: STEREOFACE - RADIOHEAD TRIBUTE
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Stereoface brings the music of Radiohead to Wellington for the first time ever! Playing music from across the band's 30 year catalogue.
Joining us will be the Bottom Bunk Club, a folkpunk/antifolk band from Pōneke, Wellington, New Zealand. They combine the energy of a sweaty basement punk gig with the feel-good plucking and strumming of an old-time bluegrass hoedown. Imagine Woody Guthrie had a baby with 90s Green Day.
And Girls Rock the Boys hit the stage with massive vocals, huge riffs, and a no‑nonsense blast of classic rock from the likes of Boston, Journey, Zeppelin and Dio. It’s loud, it’s fierce, and it’s all delivered by a powerhouse lineup of women who take these anthems and fire them straight back at the crowd. The perfect high‑voltage jolt before the night shifts into Radiohead territory.

events.id: Boney M — The Final Curtain Tour-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Boney M — The Final Curtain Tour
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s one of the most beloved bands to emerge from the golden age of disco, Boney M are back with The Final Curtain Tour, and it will feature original member Maizie Williams. The band is renowned for mixing the glitz and glamour of the 70s with catchy upbeat songs. Head to St James Theatre and enjoy all of their greatest hits. The full live band will play Rasputin, Daddy Cool, Sunny, Rivers Of Babylon, Ma Baker, Gotta Go Home, Brown Girl In The Ring and plenty more. 
Boney M recorded eight albums between 1976 and 1985, and they have gone on to sell over 150 million copies worldwide. The phenomenon continues fifty years later — here’s your last chance to sing along and relive the magic live.

events.long_description:
Oasis Centre offers Massage by appointment. During your spiritual Massage session you will feel your body, mind and soul nurtured in the most spiritual, unwinding and relaxing way. Mindful healing touch and care is given so that you can receive the full benefit of this experience.
The benefits of Massage Therapy are many and varied, and include:
•Body realignment
•Clarity for the mind
•Motivation
•Pain relief
•Rejuvenation
•Relaxation
•Relief of stress and tension
•Sense of calmness
•Therapeutic tension release
Massage is an excellent therapy for relieving pain and stiffness from the muscles of the face, scalp, neck, body, legs, upper back to the shoulders. We often use hot stones to promote deeper relaxation and muscle release.
Massage/Spiritual Healing is also an effective method to bring about a deep relaxing, enjoyable and harmonious healing. In some cases it will deal with the emotional release that is required at the time.
Come and enjoy the healing atmosphere. Have a Massage session and experience the benefits it gives to your mind, body, soul and spirit.
Each Massage session usually takes about an hour. Bookings are required as spaces are limited.
*Bookings Only*
Cost: Unfortunately we have had to set a $25 minimum on the Koha/Donation.
Phone Anne on 027 6404968

events.id: Monthly Babywearing meet up-Johnsonville Community Centre, 3 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Monthly Babywearing meet up
events.venue: Johnsonville Community Centre, 3 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join Babywearing Wellington for our monthly babywearing meet-up, open to parents, caregivers, and whānau at any stage of their babywearing journey
We are a group of experienced babywearers and qualified babywearing consultants, offering hands-on support in a relaxed, welcoming environment.
At each meet-up you can:
Try on a wide range of baby carriers from our lending library
Get help with fit and adjustments for any carriers you already own
Learn about different styles of carriers and what might suit you and your baby best
Hire a carrier to take home and try for a full month before deciding whether to buy
You can browse our full carrier library online here:
https://babywearingwellington.myturn.com/library
Hiring information:
To hire from our library, you’ll need to become a member:
$20 one-off membership fee
$10 per carrier for a month-long hire
Our meet-ups are baby and parent-friendly, and you’re welcome to come along just to learn, chat, or get a fit check — there’s no obligation to hire.
We look forward to seeing you in Room 4 (through the main doors then to your left).

events.id: FELIX BIRD + BECAUSE ROBOTS (KOHA ENTRY)-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: FELIX BIRD + BECAUSE ROBOTS (KOHA ENTRY)
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
THURS 30 APRIL | VALHALLA | 154 VIVIAN ST
Pōneke’s mid-week sanctuary for loud guitars and local legends is back. Join us at Valhalla for a double-header featuring two of the city's most distinctive voices in indie rock.
FELIX BIRD brings his signature "Haircut" sound to the stage. Navigating the space between 90s-inflected nostalgia and modern alt-rock craftsmanship, Bird and his band deliver driving arpeggios and vulnerable, clever songwriting that has quickly made them a must-watch in the local scene.
BECAUSE ROBOTS make their second ever appearance. They will be serving up a figurative buffet of sound—expect cozy indie-rock melodies layered under beautiful, staticy grit. From lo-fi intimacy to wall-of-sound distortion, it’s a set designed for the sonic explorers.
Come for the hooks, stay for the fuzz.
Thursday, 30 April
 Valhalla, 154 Vivian Street
 Doors at 8pm, Because Robots 8:30pm, Felix Bird 9:30pm
 Koha entry (Optional donation at the door)
Felix Bird | Bandcamp | Instagram
Because Robots | Bandcamp | Instagram

events.id: Seth Boy - Gold And Flowers-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Seth Boy - Gold And Flowers
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Seth Boy and his quintet present a collection of original compositions and arrangements, painting a musical portrait of the Philippines: the Land of Gold and Flowers. Born in Quezon City, and growing up in Malaybalay, Pembo, and Catalina Village, Seth brings the perspectives and sounds of Pilipinas to his evolving practice as a jazz musician, and the influence of his moving to Aotearoa as a youth.

This concert will feature native folk melodies and rhythms, Filipino popular classics, and original tunes written about concepts relating to his heritage and upbringing.

Music from 9pm, FREE ENTRY

events.id: Fat Freddy’s Drop-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Fat Freddy’s Drop
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Doing it for the love of music, Fat Freddy’s Drop takes to the Michael Fowler Centre stage in May 2026. They’ll be there for three nights performing their ground-breaking debut album, ‘Based On A True Story’. The tour will mark the first live performances since the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, “It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.” ‘Based On A True Story’ was independently released in 2005. It became Aotearoa’s longest-charting album with 111 weeks in the Top 40. It also won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.
Twenty years later, the band’s unique funk and roots style has become a defining sound of Aotearoa. “[Based On A True Story] was the band’s first full-length studio album, and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together,” explains saxophonist Scott Towers. “We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU, and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.”

events.id: James Reyne tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: James Reyne tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: The Sound
Everything has a beginning, a middle, and an end. And in February 1986, so it was for Australian Crawl.
It was a wrap. Time to sign off. A swan song, a kiss-off. Sometimes a relief, sometimes a little sad — but always resolute. Put to bed, polished off, and left behind as one of Australia’s most iconic chapters in rock history.
Now, in 2026, we mark The Fall of Crawl — a commemorative run celebrating the music, the memories, and the madness.
The songs you know. The stories you love. A reminder that time really does fly — but the soundtrack stays forever.
We can’t wait to get the band back to NZ !

events.id: HIGH ALTAR - + NEW THINGS + BOX OF HAMMERS [[MOON BAR]]-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: HIGH ALTAR - + NEW THINGS + BOX OF HAMMERS [[MOON BAR]]
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
HIGH ALTAR are an intense crackle of spectral sounds; a potent audio tool built on a bare-bones structure of rhythm section components and added electronic timbre which alights a crooked path trailing sonic vortices with oscillatory behavior. In short - drum kit, bass guitar, atmospheric electronics. Sometimes heavy, sometimes fast and punchy.
https://highaltar.bandcamp.com
THE NEW THINGS are Paekakariki-based New Zealand indie-pop known for DIY recording and melodic tunes. They bring you original indie-pop, crush songs, weird musings, and baseless libel, filtering ace rickety guitar pop with an endearing spindliness. As they put ''Our music is concise and epic, which is an oxymoron that we embrace"
https://thenewthings.bandcamp.com
BOX OF HAMMERS is an evolving, swirling maelstrom of droning guitar, mutated synths, ancient instruments and randomly discovered sounds. An imaginary soundtrack to a film that never existed.
https://boxofhammers.bandcamp.com

events.id: 1-Day Introductory Photography Workshop - Auckland-Auckland City-Facebook
events.name: 1-Day Introductory Photography Workshop - Auckland
events.venue: Auckland City
events.long_description:
This is a one-day practical hands-on workshop aimed at beginners. Designed to get you on the path to using your DSLR or mirrorless digital camera with full understanding and creative control.
We’ll help you move away from ‘Auto’ and learn how and when to use your cameras’ other modes to capture personal and unique photographs. Modern cameras are superb image-capture tools. We’ll start with a run through the important camera controls and later, in the field, learn how to bring them together. It may seem a little confusing in theory, but it will make sense when put into practice.

events.id: Turn Your Climate Concern into Confident Action for a Better World-2 Rhine Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Turn Your Climate Concern into Confident Action for a Better World
events.venue: 2 Rhine Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Feeling powerless or frustrated about the climate crisis and other global challenges?
Want to feel better, connect with like-minded people, and find easy ways to make a positive difference through small actions in your daily life?
Then join this one-day interactive workshop based on the international programme Work That Reconnects, as described in the bestselling book Active Hope by Joanna Macy and Dr Chris Johnstone.
The registration fee is only $20 and limited to 18 people. Snacks and hot drinks are provided. Participants are asked to bring a simple vegetarian lunch dish to share. The event is suitable for any adult willing to explore the following topics.

Topics
We explore the following topics through short whole-group presentations and discussions in small groups of two to four people.
· Finding Gratitude: We begin with identifying new sources of joy and strength in our lives, so we can build resilience against a widespread culture of individualism and competition.
· Transforming Despair: After morning tea we learn a psychological method for transforming powerlessness and frustration about the state of the world into confidence and hope.
· Understanding Our World: After lunch we discuss a broadened perspective on the planet's living systems to help us better understand the problems we face and find new ways forward.
· Going Forth With Hope: In the final session we identify our unique strengths and collaborative opportunities for helping ourselves and the world. From this we make personal plans for small uplifting actions that we want to take towards a better future.
See www.letstalk.wellington.govt.nz/climate-action-info-hub for examples of personal and community actions in Wellington. This workshop is supported by the Wellington City Council as part of their 2026 Climate Festival.
Visit www.activehope.info for details of a similar workshop, including free book chapters and an online course.

Facilitators Bryan Royds and Dr Ellen Miller
Bryan Royds is a leadership trainer and coach with longstanding commitment to ecological and social justice. He is an adult educator and registered counsellor by profession and has been teaching empowerment skills to groups for many years.
Ellen Miller is a GP with extensive experience in supporting psychological well-being and eco-initiatives. She cares deeply about environmental justice and has seen first-hand how concern about climate change can be channelled to strengthen people and their communities.

· Understanding Our World: After lunch we discuss a broadened perspective on the planet's living systems to help us better understand the problems we face and find new ways forward.
This workshop is supported by the Wellington City Council as part of their 2026 Climate Festival.

events.id: Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga-123 Pirie St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga
events.venue: 123 Pirie St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Looking to build healthy soils, grow healthy kai and take care of the wellbeing of your whānau, whenua and future generations?
Learn to apply hua parakore approaches in your own māra with our very special Hua Parakore Soil Health Wānanga in May.
The two-day wānanga is led by hua parakore food grower Dr Jessica Hutchings and will take place in Te Whanganui-a-Tara.
Join Jessica to learn how to:
- Reconnect and re-member soil as an ancestor - as Hineahuone.
- Explore how hua parakore supports soil health beyond productivity by focusing on balance, vitality and intergenerational care.
- Learn the six key hua parakore principles and how these inform nurturing soils without synthetic inputs.
- Understand how to observe environmental signs and work in alignment with natural and seasonal cycles.
- Reflect on your own relationships with soil and place.
- Understand how hua parakore can be applied within marae, community and whenua-based contexts to support seed, soil and food sovereignty.
For just $40, you’ll be able to join Jessica for two days, as well as enjoy kai and a resource kete.
When: Saturday 2 May 10-4pm and Sunday 3 May 10-3pm.
Where: Inner Most Gardens, Mt Victoria, Te Whanganui-a-Tara.
Spaces are limited to just 35 participants.

events.id: Hudson Freeman-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Hudson Freeman
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Hudson Freeman is a Brooklyn-based Lofi-folk artist, inspired and forged by the DIY midwest. The 27-year-old Freeman continues to record and perform songs equal-parts resonant, reflective, and poignant. He has quickly emerged as one of indie's most enthralling new voices. His recent full-length, is a Folk Artist, helped solidify his presence as the kind of songwriter unafraid to tackle life's most draining contradictions: modern identity, digital disconnection, and faith.
Born to Evangelical missionaries, Hudson started writing songs upon a radical break at the age of thirteen when his family suddenly moved from the suburbs of Dallas to The Kingdom of Eswatini. The profound influence of indie big-hitters like Sufjan Stevens and Bon Iver as well as college years spent in Springfield, Missouri made a do-it-yourself midwesterner out of Hudson. Now, his new song "If You Know Me" has recently started drawing in attention from the masses, catapulting Freeman into a wider spotlight than ever. It's a moody blend of bedroom pop, folk, slowcore, and post-emo, stitched together by a singular guitar riff and mantra-style lyrics, confirming Hudson's undeniable knack for melody.

events.id: WIDance Open and Youth Classes Term 2 2026-Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: WIDance Open and Youth Classes Term 2 2026
events.venue: Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come dance with us!
Join the WIDance team as we slide, bounce, wiggle, roll, and dance our way into Term 2, 2026!
Whether you’re a seasoned dancer or just starting out, our classes are designed to be accessible, engaging, and fun for everyone.
Our classes offer a safe and inclusive space to find joy, laughter, and creative expression through contemporary dance, improvisation, dance-based games, and composition. Through movement, we build body and space awareness, confidence, and connection.
Wellington Youth Class:
Looking for a fun, creative, and confidence-boosting activity for your young person? Our six-week Youth Dance program is designed for young people aged 7–16 (flexible) to learn, play, and grow through the art of movement.
Over the term, dancers will:
Build foundations in contemporary dance
Develop body awareness and coordination
Engage in social, team-building games and improvisation
Explore creativity with props and playful movement tasks
We celebrate individuality — each student is encouraged to move in their own way, discover new skills, and enjoy the magic of expressing themselves through dance. Whether a beginner or experienced, every dancer is welcome!
Wellington Open Class:
Our open class has been running for 15 years and is a vibrant, welcoming space where dancers of all experience levels come together to move, create, and express themselves. Each session blends technique, improvisation, and choreography, giving you room to explore your own rhythm and creativity.
This year, our technique work now includes exercises from UK based Stopgap Dance Company’s IRIS syllabus — an inclusive, dynamic approach that supports strength, awareness, and artistry for everybody.
"IRIS is an inclusive dance syllabus that gives teachers a framework to develop the disabled dancers of tomorrow. It is a consolidation of Stopgap’s experience of training professional disabled dancers, and it contains tried and tested methods that the company has been developing since 1995.
IRIS is divided into four levels: Include, Respond, Integrate and Specialise, each having its own set exercises, guidelines and assessments. IRIS will give disabled youth and adults the same level of structure, balance and rigour of dance education as their non-disabled peers and provide the opportunity to accumulate skills and experiences necessary for the professional industry".
Term 1 runs for 6 weeks, on the following dates:
Saturdays: 2nd May - 13th June (no class May 30th)
Youth Class: 10 - 11am,
Cost: $55 for the term or casual $10 per class
Open Class: 11 - 1pm
Cost: $120 waged. $85 unwaged. casual $20 per class
New option: Technique class only, 11am to 12pm, $60.
To book:
Email: sumara@widance.nz
Phone: 021 0501565
This project is proudly supported by Wellington City Council.
Who are we?
WIDance is one of Wellington's leading dance providers for disabled and non disabled dancers, striving to provide equal opportunities for all to dance. Established in 2010, we run open classes, workshops, and performance events throughout the Wellington region.
Our mission is to provide equal opportunities to dance experiences for diverse communities, advocating for access and empowerment.
#danceforall #accessforall #communitydance #disabilitydance

events.name: May Just Sew
events.venue: 2 Forresters Ln, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for our Just Sew - May edition!
Got a tear, a missing button, or a hem that’s come undone? Don’t toss it - fix it! Our monthly Just Sew sessions are all about giving your clothing and textile items a second life. This is a great way to take climate action in your own life – and learn new skills along the way.
Book below and bring along 1-2 clothing or lightweight household items (cushion covers etc) for repair. We will have sewing machines, needles, thread, and a selection of patches and buttons ready for you. If you have something special you would like to use, feel free to bring it along.
Our skilled volunteers will be here to help with repairs such as holes, tears, hems and missing buttons. Whether you are completely new to sewing or just need a bit of guidance, we will support you through it.

events.id: Best Foods Comedy Gala-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Best Foods Comedy Gala
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
There’s no better way to kick off a three-week festival of side-splitting laughs. The Best Foods Comedy Gala heralds the beginning of the NZ International Comedy Festival. For one night in Wellington, national and international comedians from the festival lineup gather to give a taster of their comedy. The fast-paced, ever-changing smorgasbord is hosted by Kiwi comedy legend Dai Henwood. He’ll introduce you to old hands and fresh voices. With every imaginable type of comedy covered, the energy is electric. Best of all, you won’t stop laughing all night.   
Dai Henwood has graced the Comedy Gala stage in previous years as a performer. This is his first time hosting. He joins a long line of Kiwi comic royalty in the role, including Hayley Sproull, Chris Parker, Melanie Bracewell, Justine Smith and Pax Assadi. Jay Law, Producer of the NZ International Comedy Festival, said the gala is like Christmas morning for comedy fans. “It’s a night where everyone can come together — artists, producers, audiences, locals, internationals — and celebrate how important comedy is.”

events.id: Public Service Broadcasting tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Public Service Broadcasting tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Handsome Tours presents the return of London-based sonic outfit Public Service Broadcasting who return to Australia and New Zealand in May, bringing their powerful, cinematic live show to audiences across the southern hemisphere.
Since emerging into existence in 2009, Public Service Broadcasting – led by instrumentalist-auteur J. Willgoose, Esq. – have forged a singular musical identity, transforming archival narratives into rousing, emotionally resonant works that blend post-rock, electronics and orchestral grandeur.
Across a celebrated catalogue including Inform-Educate-Entertain, The Race For Space, Every Valley, Bright Magic, the spectacular BBC Proms-recorded This New Noise, and their most recent work The Last Flight, the band have explored human endeavour, collective memory and the restless drive to push beyond known limits.
Sonically rich and emotionally expansive, Public Service Broadcasting’s music evokes motion, risk and wonder – from propulsive hymns of flight to moments of haunting stillness. As ever, their meticulous storytelling and immersive live performance elevate history into something urgent and deeply human.
Renowned for their visually arresting shows and ability to turn historical narratives into shared, communal experiences, Public Service Broadcasting’s return to Australia and New Zealand promises an unforgettable live encounter – one that is at once awe-inspiring, moving and exhilarating.

events.id: Ulcerate-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Ulcerate
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Via Debemur Morti:

“After triumphantly casting off the shackles of claustrophobic dissonance on 2020's lauded ‘Stare Into Death and Be Still’, New Zealand unorthodox Death Metal legends ULCERATE up the ante even further with mind-bending 7th album ‘Cutting the Throat of God’.
An acknowledgement that the band's most powerful and affecting material leans to the melodic side, this refined and hook-laden new record is a self-sufficient universe unto itself, a conflagration of inventively visceral Death/Black Metal where dizzying experimentation meets head-nodding abandon - obscenely twisted and addictive, heart-stopping in its depth of feeling.”

To complete the touring cycle for ‘Cutting the Throat of God’, the band will undertake seven Australasian headlining dates in 2026. Special guests for the New Zealand leg are the utterly deadly Vicissitude.

events.id: “All seems so lovely because all is home”: The Archaeology of Home-25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: “All seems so lovely because all is home”: The Archaeology of Home
events.venue: 25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
“All seems so lovely because all is home” wrote Katherine Mansfield to Ida Baker, April 1918.
“Home” is both a physical place, and a concept of safety, comfort, and belonging.
In her work as an archaeologist Mary O'Keeffe has explored the idea of “home”: what places and areas in New Zealand have different people called home over the centuries of occupation, and how and why has this changed.
In this illustrated talk, Mary will show the stories and small details revealed by archaeology that shape our ideas of what “home” is.
Part of New Zealand Archaeology Week 2026 and in conjunction with our current exhibition, 'Uncovering Katherine Mansfield's House'. This event is free but, as a charity, donations are always appreciated.
Registration is required due to limited space in the house. Please note that this event will take place upstairs, see the Accessibility page of our website for more information.
You can also attend this event online via Zoom.
Image: Fragments found during archaeological work at Katherine Mansfield House & Garden, reconstructed into a cup. Photo by Kevin Jones.

TOGETHER also commemorates two important modern union movement leaders, Helen Kelly and Peter Conway.
events.description:
Presented by: UnionAID TOGETHER is an evening of music, celebration and solidarity with Don McGlashan, Tiny Ruins, SJD, Sofia Machray, Luke Buda (The Phoenix Foundation), Ross Burge and David Long (Muttonbirds) performing songs.
Some of New Zealand’s top orchestral musicians will be performing too.
All money raised will go to UnionAid — supporting economic justice and social development projects across the Asia Pacific region.
TOGETHER also commemorates two important modern union movement leaders, Helen Kelly and Peter Conway.

events.id: Out in the Pool-15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Out in the Pool
events.venue: 15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our Out in the Pool sessions are regular monthly sessions for all queer, transgender, gender diverse and non-binary folks
We invite our rainbow communities and allies to dive in and swim in a safe and welcoming environment at Keith Spry Pool. These sessions are held on the first Sunday of every month and run 5pm-7pm, starting slightly later in February with our first event on Sunday 8 February 2026.
Out in the Pool evenings at Keith Spry have:
Gender neutral changing rooms and bathrooms, as well as our Men's and Women's changing facilities.
Recreation lanes with inflatables, as well as the Dive board open (as staffing allows).
Lane space will be available to swim some lengths or aqua jog, and the teaching pool will be open as staffing allows.
Spa and Sauna available
Music, swimming and good vibes only.
There is no additional cost for these sessions, simply turn up and pay our regular pool entry fees. There is no need to register, just come on down!

events.id: Perinatal Mental Health Awareness Week | 4-10 May 2026--41.211111, 174.781111-Facebook
events.name: Perinatal Mental Health Awareness Week | 4-10 May 2026
events.long_description:
In 2026, Perinatal Mental Health Awareness Week (PMHAW) will be recognised from Monday 4 May to Sunday 10 May, aligning the week with World Maternal Mental Health Day which falls on the first Wednesday in May every year.
This year’s theme is Walking With You.
Real support. Real listening. Real change.
This year we are asking buildings and landmarks to light up green for PMHAW in recognition of perinatal mental health. You can find the confirmed list here at pada.nz/perinatal-mental-health-awareness-week/
If you, your workplace, or organisation plan to participate, we would love to hear from you.

events.id: Prediabetes (04/05/26) Otaki-Otaki-Facebook
events.name: Prediabetes (04/05/26) Otaki
events.venue: Otaki
events.long_description:
Have you been told you have prediabetes?
Are you wondering what you can do to help manage it? If so, the MHT Diabetes Trust can help.
In this FREE 2 hour course you will learn:
- What is prediabetes
- How you can manage it with food, activity and lifestyle choices
- How you can reduce your risk of developing type 2 diabetes.
Please register by visiting our website www.diabetestrust.org.nz and select BOOK A COURSE, then select the Pre-Diabetes label and click on the course you would like to attend. Complete your booking by selecting BOOK NOW. It is important to register so that we know how many participants there are so that we can provide adequate resources and catering.
We look forward to seeing you there!

events.id: Baxter Dury tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Baxter Dury tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Following the release of his latest album, UK alternative artist Baxter Dury is bringing his Allbarone Tour 2026 to Australia and New Zealand. With genre-blurring music that blends post-punk, indietronica and art-rock, his sound leans on sleek synths and a cool, understated aesthetic rooted in a distinctly British sensibility. ‘Allbarone’ can be described as a nine-track tour-de-force, stripping everything away and building Baxter’s most melodically direct, futuristic collection yet.
The Australian leg of the tour will kick off at Melbourne’s Forum on April 28, before heading to Roundhouse in Sydney on April 29, and concluding at The Tivoli in Brisbane on May 1. Dury will then head to New Zealand for performances at The Hollywood Avondale in Auckland on May 3 and Wellington’s Meow Nui on May 4.

events.id: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
English in the Community: Johnsonville - Beginners+ (ESOL Level 1-2)
English in the Community courses help you to learn more English, get to know your community and meet new people. The groups aim to help you use what you already know, learn more from your tutor and the other students and feel more at home in your local community. 
To enrol in these courses, please contact the CEC office on 04 385 8919 or cec@whs.school.nz.
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Feel more confident speaking English in everyday situations 
• Be able to understand more English 
• Have learned new words and basic grammar
• Know more about living in New Zealand and your local community
The courses are free for NZ residents and citizens. The course fee for non-residents is $155.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
This is an introduction to the wonderful, organic material that is clay. You'll receive a comprehensive overview to working with clay which will cover all steps from clay preparation and re-cycling through to glazing the finished work. You'll learn to throw basic forms with group demonstration and one-to-one instruction.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
•  Wedge and re-cycle clay 
•  Demonstrate the basics of working on a pottery wheel 
•  Centre clay on the wheel 
•  Open a centred piece of clay on wheel 
•  Pull clay up to form basic beaker/cylinder shape 
•  Demonstrate the correct use of prepared glazes 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Communication English - Level 2-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Communication English - Level 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Communication English - Level 2
For those who can understand and use simple English in everyday situations but need to build confidence, vocabulary and grammar. 
By the end of the course, you'll:
• Feel more confident speaking English in everyday situations
• Be able to write more correct sentences
• Be able to understand more English, especially “New Zealand English”
• Have increased your vocabulary
• Be able to describe more about living in New Zealand and New Zealand culture
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents but to enrol you must contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) for an interview. There are limited free places available and you may need to go on a waiting list.  
There may be some places on the course for people who are not NZ residents or citizens but you must pay the course fee of $1049. 
This course is subsidised by the Tertiary Education Commission and you must provide evidence of your NZ residency or citizenship (if applicable).
Please note that there is no student parking at the High School during the day. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
Learn how to prepare and alter clay:  pinch, coil, slab, extruder and roller use.  Understand shrinkage and clay care, joining techniques, drape and press moulding, mark making, slip making and application - as well as tetrahedron, cone and cylinder production
The techniques learnt during the making will provide an excellent springboard to creating a wide array of ceramic objects in the future.
The emphasis will be on learning techniques and honing those skills and by the end of the 8 sessions you will have completed three works which will be fired and glazed.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
A follow-on course for those who have some experience of throwing on the wheel and would like some guidance to take their skills to the next stage.
You’ll learn how to “turn” your pots with more confidence and be able to make a variety of small to medium-sized projects. Basic glazing of your pots will also be covered.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Turn a pot
• Make a lidded container
• Make a rounded vase
• Form a reasonable sized bowl
• Do basic glazing
All materials included in course fee. Wear old clothes and footwear suitable for a workshop environment and bring an old towel.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $79 with ID, all others pay $149).
An opportunity to learn basic practical skills to assist you in learning to communicate in an assertive healthy manner. Come prepared to participate in class, as well as be working on assigned activities between classes. We'll work both individually and as a group using this environment to help us grow as we learn.
  By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
Use a strategy to identify your own feelings, and to express what you would like
Have an increased understanding of your own and other peoples’ anger, dealing with that in a more assertive manner
Use strategies to allow you not to be manipulated by other people
Speak in a way that commands respect and to begin to learn how to say “no”
Begin interacting more effectively in your personal relationships and the workplace
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
Come along and learn how to create wonderful Vietnamese dishes bursting with fresh flavours. 
Depending on what's seasonally available, dishes taught may include some of the following: green papaya prawn salad (Nộm tôm đu đủ xanh), lemongrass chicken stir fry with steamed rice (Cơm Gà rang sả) , grilled pork noodle and fried rolls (bún nem thịt nướng) and beef noodle soup (Phở bò). 
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya
Thai cuisine is an exciting explosion of tastes and textures. This highly aromatic food has become popular around the globe. Each week you will be shown different Thai dishes and you will get to make these. This course will provide you with tips, tricks and knowledge and it will be fun, easy and yummy!
Not suitable for vegetarians.  All ingredients included in course fee – please bring to each session your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Make a range of Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients spices and herbs like Thai people do!
Be able to explain some simple Thai eating etiquette
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
ESOL - Level 1-2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $155 with ID, all others pay $305).
By the end of the course, you'll:
Feel more confident speaking simple English in everyday situations
Have learned more new words and improved your pronunciation
Have learned new words and basic grammar
Be able to understand and talk about life in New Zealand
If you are enrolling with us for the first time, please contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) before you enrol on-line so that we can check the level of your English to make sure that you are in the correct class.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
NZ Sign Language - Introductory 1A
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $205 applies.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to communicate in basic sign language and will be able to:

• Express basic needs and wants
• Use a basic signing vocabulary

events.id: Sewing - All Levels-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sewing - All Levels
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sewing - All Levels
All levels welcome! Beginning sewers will learn basic skills and techniques and those with some experience will improve their skills and learn tips and tricks.
Beginners - If you're a beginner, you'll start by making a lined bag, which teaches all the basic skills involved. After that you'll move on to a project of your choice, in consultation with the tutor.  Please bring: 1 metre of firm cotton fabric for your bag outer and 1 metre of a lighter cotton fabric for the lining. You'll also need to bring scissors, a Bernina bobbin (this can be purchased from the CEC office on the day of the course for $7), pins, thread to match your outer fabric, an 18 cm zip and tape measure to the first session.
 If you're a returning student, or have already mastered the basic skills, you'll work on your own project so please bring along whatever you're currently working on (or something new) and your sewing kit. 
Please note PDF patterns are not suitable for this course unless you are very experienced in using them;  we strongly recommend patterns from the following companies:  Simplicity, Butterick, New Look or Quicksew.
By the end of the course, you'll have been introduced to basic skills in dressmaking, if beginners, or further skills for the more experienced.
You'll be able to:
• Use all sewing machine equipment and demonstrate basic sewing techniques (basic seams and finishes)
• Begin to read a commercial pattern and select appropriate materials for task
• Cut out and construct a garment using techniques learned
• (In addition), use a variety of advanced sewing techniques e.g. rolled hems, various zip insertions (invisible and standard), make buttonholes or line garments. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Te Reo Māori - Introductory 1
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $110 with ID, all others pay $205).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Have a basic understanding of the Māori spiritual and natural worlds (mana atua, mana whenua), and our important connections to them
• Identify the sounds of Māori letters and pronounce letter combinations using correct intonation and stress patterns
• Confidently greet people, introduce yourself and communicate basic personal information in an informal context
• Identify and respond to phrases in the classroom including basic commands/instructions
• Confidently describe the day/date, time, and the weather

We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Japanese - Introductory 2-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Japanese - Introductory 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Japanese - Introductory 2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $105 with ID, all others pay $205).
For those who have done Introductory 1 (16 hours) or have equivalent knowledge (e.g. who may have picked up some basic survival language through travel).
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
Understand past tense
Talk about your experience.
Talk about what you can do.
Understand the difference between “いく”, ”くる” and ”かえる”
Understand the difference between “あげる”, ”くれる” and ”もらう”
Recognise some counters and 80 kanji.
Ask and give direction.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
(Images courtesy of Deb Scheib Photography)

events.id: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings
In this exciting beginners course you'll be guided to make a set of 3 cast rings embedded with precious gemstones!  The course follows similar skills and steps as our Lost Wax Cast Rings class, plus a range of sapphires, rubies & other robust stones to add to your rings, 'cast-in-place'.
This beginner-friendly jewellery class is also fun and engaging for returning students to diversify their skills and set stones in new ways.
All materials are supplied in your booking so you won't end up with surprise costs.
Included in your course:
Use of a range of jewellery wax types to create 2 practice rings then 1  main project ring
5 facet-cut precious gemstones to set across your 3 rings (choose from rubies, spinels and sapphires in a range of colours)
Industry standard casting services that we will send your wax models away to so they can be transformed into brass, bronze and sterling silver rings
2 original practice rings cast in bronze or brass
1 main project ring cast in Sterling Silver
During sessions 1-3 you will be guided to sculpt a range of wax ring models and embed precious gemstones, finishing one ring each session ready for casting. 
Between classes your waxes will be transformed into rings for you. You can choose from bronze or yellow brass for your first two rings. These first 2 rings are a great opportunity to practice techniques and experiment with shapes, textures and designs before making your main project ring which will be cast in sterling silver!
In the second half of this course you will discover techniques to trim casting sprues, file, sand, refine and polish your casts into finished jewellery.
The bold, organic forms and textures of the lost wax processes offer a wide range of creative possibilities beyond the scope of fabrication methods alone (soldering metal sheet and wire). Setting faceted stones in wax offers a simpler and more experimental way of adding stones to jewellery compared to fabrication settings. methods. Casdt jewellery techniques and fabrication jewellery techniques can also be combined to great effect, which makes this course a fantastic option for beginners and experienced makers alike.

All materials are included in the course fee. Please bring pen and paper and your reading glasses (if you use these) and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.

By the end of the course you will be able to:
Evolve your jewellery making abilities with new and exciting klost-wax methods
Design and create original sculptural ring designs
Carve hard jeweller’s wax ring master designs to fit a chosen finger size for casting
Sculpt soft jeweller’s wax master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting
Form soft jeweller’s wax to create unique designs ready for casting
Set faceted gemstones in wax for ‘cast-in-place’ setting methods
File, sand and polish metal casts into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $110 with ID, all others pay $205).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Discuss our responsibilities as a bi-cultural nation under ‘Te Tiriti o Waitangi’ and identify underlying Māori values such as: whanaungatanga, pepehā and tikanga
• Discuss the significance of whakapapa as a fundamental Māori world view and confidently describe your family and relationships through this lens
• Form simple sentences to describe parts of your face and body
• Discuss protocol on the marae
• Recognise the importance and use of karakia and be able to recite one correctly
• Recognise the cultural values inherent within different whakataukī (proverbs) and kīwaha (idioms) and be able to correctly recite one of each
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Tuning in to Teens-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Tuning in to Teens
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tuning in to Teens™ – Strengthen Your Connection, Empower Your Teen
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
 ‘Tuning in to Teens’ is led by educational psychologist Vernice Young, this evidence based programme focuses on Emotion Coaching, helping parents:

✅ Understand the neuroscience behind teen emotions and development
✅ Build stronger, more connected relationships
✅ Reduce tension and conflict at home
✅ Support teens in managing emotions, peer pressure, and relationships
Each session introduces practical techniques you’ll apply between classes, ensuring real-life impact.
Invest in your relationship -tune in to your teen.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Ukulele - Beginners
Have fun (earn chocolate!) and learn to play common chords and strum patterns.
Please note your ukulele must be tuned to standard C tuning.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Play the most useful and common chords: C, F, G7, G and D
• Play common strum patterns at a basic level
• Make basic chord changes
• Play at least eight “campfire” songs at a basic level
• Tune your instrument
• Play in other keys using more chords
Bring your ukulele. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
