events.id: Mosaic Design-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mosaic Design
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mosaic Design
You'll be able to make several pieces of mosaic to your own design and after the completion of the course, will feel confident to carry on working on your own projects. 
A list of materials and tools required (approximate cost $70, depending what you may already have at home) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. Please wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.
By the end of the four-week course, you'll be able to:
• Demonstrate the basic principles/techniques of mosaic design
• Break and cut tiles correctly
• Place tiles on the object
• Stick pieces to flat and curved surfaces
• Grout a mosaic piece
• Use stones and shells in mosaics
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Drawing for Beginners
A back-to-basics programme for those who always wanted to know how to draw but never had the chance to learn.
Bring along your own sketchbook or visual diary (A4 or larger) All other course materials will be provided.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
•  Use line to draw and gain familiarity with using a pencil and other drawing mediums, including oil pastel, coloured pencil and charcoal
•  Develop observational skills to aid the drawing process and practise line drawing techniques
•  Use tone and shading to describe form and light
•  Create a final drawing of your choice
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Scottish Country Dancing-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Scottish Country Dancing
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Scottish Country Dancing
Why do Scottish Country Dancing? Great music and amazing ‘brain gym’ for body and mind! Scottish Country Dancing is a very social form of dancing, danced in "sets" usually with four "couples" - unlike Highland Dancing which is usually solo dancing. Have fun and meet new people, while getting fit!
It’s suitable for all levels of fitness, and is a great way of meeting and socialising with new people. Scottish country dancing is a common pastime for thousands of dancers in many countries, whether you’re in Japan, Germany, the US or Scotland itself.
Wear soft flat shoes and loose clothing and bring a bottle of water. No partner is needed – we change partners for every dance!
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Name and perform to beginner level the five major steps of Scottish Country Dancing
• Name and perform ten simple formations
• Participate in five simple Scottish Country dances, in strathspey (slow) and reel/jig (fast) tempo
• Clap rhythm to reel, jig and strathspey tempo
• Have the confidence and skills to join a club to progress your social dancing 
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
During their recording career, Split Enz released a string of hits across the late 70s and early 80s. Those songs quickly gained notoriety and defined an era for many New Zealanders. Now, for the first time in nearly 20 years, the band has reunited for a New Zealand tour. Featuring original members Tim Finn, Neil Finn, Eddie Rayner and Noel Crombie, the Split Enz — Forever Tour will remind audiences of the band’s huge creative talent. Songs include I Got You, I See Red, Message To My Girl, Six Months In A Leaky Boat, History Never Repeats, One Step Ahead, and plenty more. 
Formed in New Zealand in 1972 before relocating to Australia in 1975, Split Enz is widely regarded as one of the most important antipodean groups of the last five decades. Their number one, 1980 album release, “True Colours”, achieved multiple-platinum status in Aotearoa and was certified five times platinum in Australia, selling over 250,000 copies. In 2005, Split Enz were inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame. Says founding member Tim Finn — “Aotearoa, rugged individual! We’re looking forward to climbing into our new Noel Crombie suits and seeing you all soon.”

events.id: Thundercat tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Thundercat tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Bench Music
Known for his jazz-fusion sensibility and elastic funk, Thundercat is bringing his 2026 tour to Wellington this May, performing live in support of his new album Distracted. See his show at Meow NUI in Wellington on Wed, 6 May 2026.
In an age of endless notifications and fractured attention, Thundercat turns the chaos inward on Distracted, his long-awaited new album and one of his most resonant statements yet. Six years on from It Is What It Is, the record captures the restless aftermath of grief and modern overload, balancing existential weight with Thundercat’s unmistakable wit and warmth. It’s an album that laughs through the noise, folding jazz-fusion virtuosity, elastic funk and late-night introspection into songs that feel like unfiltered inner monologues: tender, funny, and painfully human.
That philosophy carries directly into the live experience. Onstage, Thundercat treats Distracted not as a lecture on modern life, but as a shared release, stretching songs, breaking the fourth wall, leaning into humour and spontaneity as a form of survival.
Thundercat's New Zealand shows promise moments of presence inside the noise: music that drifts, refocuses, and reminds you why joy still matters. It’s playful, loose, deeply human, and a rare chance to experience Distracted and all of Thundercat the way it was meant to be felt, not scrolled past.
Thundercat - 'I Did This To Myself (feat. Lil Yachty)' (Official Audio)
Thundercat - 'I Wish I Didn't Waste Your Time'
Thundercat - 'No Moe Lies (feat. Tame Impala)' (Official Visualizer)

events.long_description:
Leadlighting/Stained Glass
Bring along your project ideas and learn the techniques of glass cutting and making stained glass panels. Suitable for both beginners and those with previous knowledge.
NOTE: If this is your first Leadlighting course, you MUST attend the first session when key instruction is given. If you do not attend the compulsory first session, you will forfeit your course fees and will not be able to transfer to a future session of this course
(Unsuitable for pregnant people due to presence of lead in some materials)
 Your materials costs are dependent on the size of your project and the coloured glass content (likely minimum cost around $170). Please wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.
By the end of the course, you'll: 
• Have designed and constructed a small rectangle/window, lampshade or light catcher 
• Be able to cut glass and assemble leadlighting panels 
• Be able to solder, seal and finish a leadlighting project 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
We will prepare simple, traditional Japanese home-style meals consisting of rice, soup, and a main dish (Ichijyu-issai). In each class, we'll make a different-flavoured miso soup along with a popular Japanese dish -- for example, teriyaki chicken or tonkatsu (crumbed pork cutlet).
  We'll introduce you to Japanese food that's easy to prepare, tasty, and made with simple fresh ingredients plus we'll show you how easy it is to incorporate tasty and healthy Japanese food into your daily diet.
All ingredients are included in the fee. You just need to bring an apron, a dish cloth, a tea towel, and a container to take your food home in. Don't forget your smartphone if you want to take photos!
By the end of the course, you’ll:
become more familiar with using Japanese ingredients
feel more confident to cook authentic Japanese-style dishes at home 
have the know-how to make delicious, well-balanced, and nourishing meals for the whole family.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus. 
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Creative Writing - Express Yourself-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - Express Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - Express Yourself
Creative writing is a powerful tool for personal growth and is an art form that belongs to all people. Everyone has their own unique writer’s voice, so if you’ve always wanted to explore your creativity through writing, without pressure, join this weekly workshop.
  No previous writing experience needed - express yourself! Bring pen and paper and/or your laptop.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Unlock your hidden creativity
• Explore your writer’s voice
• Experience personal insights
• Develop self-awareness through writing
• Produce text with confidence
• Write on 
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers' work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Life Drawing - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Life Drawing - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Life Drawing - Beginners
This course will help artists and designers at all levels to develop their life-drawing skills. Starting with an exploration of structure, form, shape and proportion, we will progress through foreshortening while creating illusions of depth and atmosphere using light sourcing. You will also learn to draw using a range of media.
Please bring an A2 sketch pad, 2B and 4B pencils and an eraser.  Cost of model included in course fee.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
- Draw the human form
- Comprehend structure and shape
- Demonstrate fundamental drawing skills
- Be familiar with proportion
- Use drawing media to create tonal range
Please note this course uses live models to draw from. It may include some nudity, including draped models and possibly completely unclothed models. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Smartphone Video & Editing
Smartphone videography is an increasingly important and useful skillset. Whether you fancy yourself as a social media influencer or you want to make and edit content for your business for life – this course will teach you how to plan video content, use a variety of camera techniques and employ basic video principles. We will also explore editing apps and how to produce polished videos for platforms like YouTube, Tik Tok and Facebook. 
Bring your smartphone to each session.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
By the end of the course, you will have a solid understanding of:
*Know of and be able to use some editing apps
*Be able to use the basic principles of videography
*Create workable content ideas for videos
*Perform basic camera techniques
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Make Your Own Roman Blinds-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Make Your Own Roman Blinds
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make Your Own Roman Blinds
Roman blinds are a perennial favourite because they use a lot less fabric than the usual pencil pleated or gathered curtains - as well as making your rooms look streamlined and uncluttered.
During the four weeks you will learn how to accurately measure your window to enable you to create very own customized roman blind. 
Some hardware included in course fee but a list of all other materials (fabric, etc) will be sent out to you when the course is confirmed.
Please choose a narrow window for your first project - max 150cms wide.
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Accurately measure your window 
Calculate how much fabric to purchase
Measure and cut out your curtain
Use appropriate machine skills to sew your curtain fabric
Use finishing skills to apply rings and cords to your curtain
Identify appropriate method to hang Roman Blind
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Kyla Cobbler — Gone Rogue-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Kyla Cobbler — Gone Rogue
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Known for raw storytelling, razor-sharp crowd work, and unfiltered honesty, Kyla Cobbler commands the stage with total confidence. The comic from Cork in Ireland brings her show ‘Gone Rogue’ to Wellington. In it, Kyla covers everything from the chaos of Barcelona dating life, navigating a friend crush, and the power plays of a barbecue. 
Kyla is one of the fastest-rising names in comedy. She first gained attention through her viral Instagram reels before stepping on stage for her first open mic in 2021. Five years later, Kyla has built a global following, with close to 700K Instagram followers and 100M+ video views. 
Kyla brings quickfire wit and a knack for spinning real-life chaos into killer stories. Get ready for a performance that is electric, unpredictable, and unforgettable.

events.name: DJ Music Production - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
DJ Music Production - An Introduction
Welcome to the world of Electronic Dance Music (EDM). This introductory course will show you the "ins and outs" of producing your own music. Using Fruity Loops SL as the main production program, you'll learn how to compose, arrange, record, edit, mix and master quality music.
While it's designed to complement the "How to be A DJ" course, you can still enrol if you have no experience. 
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Use the basics of the Fruity Loops SL software
- Navigate audio and midi tracks
- Create drum Lines and sequences
- Play basslines and samples
- Render tracks and save formats e.g. mp3, wav etc
- Use basic mixdown and master of music
The course will be held at DJ Shan’s studio at Shop 21, 73 Cuba Street, Wellington.  Bring headphones, a USB and a laptop, if you have one. Limited to eight participants.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
Elevate your photography from ordinary to extraordinary and ensure your work stands out in the digital age. Creating captivating photographs and understanding what makes a photograph engaging is something every photographer should master. Camera settings are essential, but once you know them, you need to learn how to take photographs that stir emotions, impact your audience, and leave a lasting impression. After all, isn’t that the true purpose of photography? People don’t want to see the same landscapes or flowers they’ve seen thousands of times. But how do you turn what you love into something that makes people go, "Wow"?
Captivation in photography means stopping a viewer in their tracks, making them look, feel, and connect. It’s about delivering a photograph that enthrals, evokes profound emotions, and shifts perspectives.
In a world brimming with images, only a few have the power to truly captivate and linger in our memories.
 This pioneering course is your gateway to mastering the art of creating captivating photographs at a time when AI-generated images are saturating our visual landscape. Create something genuine and memorable—images that spark curiosity, evoke emotions, and inspire changes in perception. It's time to define your unique style, refine your vision, and make your mark.
 Course Outcomes
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
Identify the foundational elements that transform an ordinary image into a captivating one.
Create photographs that make people say "Wow" by applying techniques that add a spark of wonder to your shots.
Infuse your photographs with curiosity, making each frame a source of intrigue and interest.
Understand why some images linger in memory and how to make your photographs unforgettable.
Develop a unique style and vision that sets your work apart in the crowded digital landscape.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Garden Design: Landscaping-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Garden Design: Landscaping
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Garden Design: Landscaping
In this fun and interactive class you will be inspired to find answers to develop a simple makeover or design a grand new plan for your own outdoor area. Find solutions to problems and with creativity, develop your own design to suit your site and character.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
 
• Anticipate what hardscaping is required and draw up a full plan for a garden / outdoor space.
• Identify and take into account your preferred style, wish list and work to site conditions (including location and aspect of the property.)
• Accurately draw your detailed design on paper and to scale.
• Use your drawing to build the garden yourself or use it to consult a builder/landscaper to do the hardscaping on your behalf.
• Identify what materials will produce the style / preferred end result
• Work to a budget or know what range of budget to anticipate
 
Please note that this course DOES NOT include:
 
• Planting design, although inclusion of trees will be considered in part of this course. Planting design is expected to be available in a later course.
• Building the garden
• Learning skills to build your garden
• Recommendation of contractors and suppliers for hardscaping

Please bring some scrap paper, a notebook, pens, pencils and an eraser.
 
It is highly recommended you take photos of the area. Bring these in to discuss in class as “personal case studies”.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Lost Wax Cast Rings
The lost wax process offers a wide range of creative outcomes such as 3D forms and textures, which are not achievable with fabrication soldering techniques.
You'll get a hands-on opportunity to develop new techniques creating rings with a sculptural and organic flair.
All materials are supplied and included, so you won't end up with surprise "extra" costs.
Included in your course:
Explore using a range of jewellery wax types to create 2 practice rings and 1 X main project ring
Access to industry-standard casting services where we will send your  wax models to be transformed into brass, bronze and sterling silver rings 
2 original practice rings cast in bronze or yellow brass
1 main project ring cast in sterling silver
In sessions 1, 2 and 3 you will be guided to sculpt a range of wax ring models, finishing one each session so it is ready for casting. Between classes your waxes will be transformed into rings for you. You can choose from bronze or yellow brass for your first two rings. These first 2 rings are a great opportunity to practice techniques and experiment with shapes, textures and designs before making your main project ring which will be cast in sterling silver!
In the second half of this course you will discover techniques to trim casting sprues, file, sand, refine and polish your casts into finished jewellery.
This course offers beginners a fun introduction to jewellery making, and for those with some jewellery experience, it offers a great way to advance and expand your making techniques to new levels!
All materials are included in course fee.  Please bring pen and paper and your reading glasses (if you use these) and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.  
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-          Evolve your jewellery making abilities with new and exciting lost wax methods
-          Develop and create unique sculptural ring designs
-          Carve hard jeweller’s wax ring master designs to fit a chosen finger size for casting
-          Form soft jeweller’s wax master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting
-          Combine hard and soft jeweller’s wax to create unique designs ready for casting
-          File, sand and polish metal casts into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Improv for the Stage-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Improv for the Stage
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Improv for the Stage
Laugh, play characters, get them into trouble (and out again!) and see how your skills translate into confident performance.
This is the WIT Level 2 course and will take your improv that next step beyond the confidence-building of our Joy of Improv, and on into collaborative spontaneous theatre creation.
Suitable for those who have completed a foundation course either with WITor another improv troupe or have some theatrical experience or training.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Create characters and stories in open scenes
• Play and perform several classic improv games
• Stay positive and enjoy failure
• Use improvisation terminology to discuss story, character, comedy, theatre and think about performance (your own and others)
The course will end with a show for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Photography - Beginners
If you want to get the best results from your camera, you need to learn the fundamental skills and theory of good photography. 
Bring your fully manual-capable (D)SLR or mirrorless camera and owner's manual (if you have one) to the first session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Operate your camera in manual mode with more confidence 
• Use your camera controls such as shutter speed and aperture with confidence

• Present and discuss images in a group environment

events.id: The Joy of Improvisation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: The Joy of Improvisation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Joy of Improvisation
Improv is theatre without a script and a fun way to build your skills in many areas, including team work, public speaking, storytelling and on-your-feet creative thinking.
This is the perfect place for absolute beginners to get acquainted with the joy and art of improvisation, inspired by the generous creative philosophy of Keith Johnstone. It's also a great place to get back into improv if you've been away from it for a while.
WIT has been teaching this course in association with the CEC for almost 20 years now. It uses fun games and structured exercises to teach the foundation skills of improv. These are a prerequisite for further courses and membership of WIT (www.wit.org.nz), as well as useful in themselves.
If you have questions about the course, please contact WIT directly on info@wit.org.nz.
By the end of the course, you'll have: 
• Explored basic principles of improvisation
• Enjoyed interacting in a friendly, positive and supportive environment
• Developed more confidence, presentation and communication skills
• Experienced joyful moments of improvisation
The course will end with a directed demonstration of class exercises for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Funky Mama has been a staple in the Wellington music diet since its inception in 2016.

Fresh from curing cases of deep vein trombosis in Tauranga, they bring their signature sound that's influenced by deep funk, bouncy boogaloo & 80's house.

This will be their last gig for a wee while as their leader and Saxophonist, Bryn van Vliet, is leaving these shores for larger and most likely greyer pastures.

Come and have a Thursday Night Libation and get your deep vein trombosis cured by the best in the biz.

Music from 9:00pm, FREE ENTRY

events.id: Featherston Booktown Karukatea Festival--Wellington NZ
events.name: Featherston Booktown Karukatea Festival
events.long_description:
The Aotearoa literary landscape is placed front and centre at Featherston Booktown. The annual book festival is now in its eleventh year and expects to attract 10,000 to the south Wairarapa town from 8 to 10 May.
The lineup of writers, raconteurs, and thinkers includes renowned Australian author Hannah Kent, Aotearoa superstar Catherine Chidgey, and activist Tāme Iti. You’ll also find former Olympian and popular reading influencer Gemma Flynn, and former All Blacks mental skills coach Gilbert Enoka. Director Jordan Hamel says he is thrilled to bring the festival into its second decade. “There’s an exciting programme, with bold conversations, new innovations, and an incredible array of national and international talent.”

The annual book festival is now in its eleventh year and expects to attract 10,000 to the south Wairarapa town from 8 to 10 May.
The lineup of writers, raconteurs, and thinkers includes renowned Australian author Hannah Kent, Aotearoa superstar Catherine Chidgey, and activist Tāme Iti.
You’ll also find former Olympian and popular reading influencer Gemma Flynn, and former All Blacks mental skills coach Gilbert Enoka.
Director Jordan Hamel says he is thrilled to bring the festival into its second decade.

events.id: Tom Scott tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Tom Scott tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Off the back of the release of his Aotearoa Music Chart topping album ANITYA, Tom Scott brings his self untitled tour to Wellington to play Meow Nui on Friday, 8 May.
Raw as exposed nerves after a car crash, and as tender as the final kiss goodbye, ANITYA is the first solo album from Tom Scott, one of the most acclaimed New Zealand artists of his generation, hip-hop or otherwise. Recorded with a cast of 30 of the most versatile musicians, beatmakers and vocalists in his contact list and the award-winning studio engineer and producer Ben Lawson, it represents a significant creative breakthrough for one of Avondale’s finest. A tale told in two halves, ANITYA is a collect call from the void, offering redemption as you gaze into the house fire. Rendered through a nocturnal blend of progressive soul, dream-pop, jazzy psychedelic folk and ambient R&B, the album’s ten songs confirm Scott’s status as a preternaturally gifted lyricist with an ear for a unifying hook, revelling in the expansive possibilities of who he is still becoming as a songwriter, producer and melodist.
It’s not all pretty, but it’s high art. After all, much like the truth, art can be very ugly.
Born of the blood, sweat, and tears shed with Homebrew, @Peace, Average Rap Band, and Avantdale Bowling Club, Scott’s craft as a music maker and performer is second to none.

events.id: & Juliet-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: & Juliet
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
What might have happened if Shakespeare’s Romeo and Juliet had ended differently? A ‘what if’ scenario is given life in a new musical coming to Wellington in 2026. & Juliet explores the idea of what the Bard’s heroine might have done if she’d lived beyond the end of the play. The bold reimagining uses fantastic songs to tell a story through a fiercely independent lens. The result is a fabulous, feel-good journey of self-discovery, empowerment, and second chances. 
& Juliet is a jukebox musical. That means the story is told using a playlist of pop bangers you already know and love. It features chart-toppers like Since U Been Gone, Roar, Baby One More Time, Larger Than Life, and Can’t Stop the Feeling! The show pulses with energy, heart, and non-stop fun. 
You’ll be wowed with dazzling costumes, electrifying choreography, and hit after hit. The retelling of a classic love story through a bold new lens makes & Juliet more than just a musical. It’s a celebration of rewriting your own narrative.

events.long_description:
Presented by: Your Favourite Band
Your Favourite Band (YFB) is proud to present The Music of Kendrick Lamar: Reimagined Live.
Titans of Aotearoa music celebrating one of hip hop’s most influential voices.
Kendrick Lamar (K-Dot, Kung Fu Kenny, Cornrow Kenny, oklama) is known for pushing boundaries—blending sharp storytelling with jazz, funk, soul, and experimental hip hop, and unquestionably reshaping the sound of modern hip-hop.
This show flips Kendrick’s catalogue into a live setting, reworking key tracks with new textures, live vocal samples, heavy grooves, and room to stretch out and improvise. Performed by members of renowned boundary pushing bands in their own right Avantdale Bowling Club and Yoko-Zuna.
Don’t miss out on this homage to one of modern music’s most important voices at Wellington’s own Meow Nui.
Support to be announced.

Join cake expert, Lina, for a hands-on cake building and decorating workshop where creativity meets craftsmanship!

 Learn how to banish stage fright and performance anxiety. Communicate from a place of confidence and energy. Hold the room and speak with authority. Come across authentically, with maximum personal impact.

events.id: Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: CV and Cover Letters
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Explore how to appropriately structure and polish your CV and cover letter in order to achieve optimum results. This course prioritises you. Identify your key skills and weave these into your CV and cover letter to land you your next job interview.
 Whatever job you are going for, there are key characteristics that should not be missed in your CV and cover letter. The focus of this course is to provide you with the same lens as a recruiter. By learning how your job application is reviewed by a recruiter, you will gain skills to streamline your CV, and improve your cover letter writing. This will increase your chances of being a successful candidate and sets you up to land an interview for your ideal job.
 It doesn’t matter what previous experience you have - everyone has a range of skills that they can offer to their future employer. Fortunately, a strong and realistic CV and cover letter will showcase these skills and increase your chances for job success.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Recognise the features of an effective, well-crafted CV and cover letter and apply these to your own job applications;
- Apply different approaches, styles and techniques while writing your CV and cover letter depending on the context and job;
- Help others with their CV and cover letter writing (friends and whānau).
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making - Faceted Gemstone Settings
In this challenging and exciting workshop students will learn to set glimmering round-cut faceted gemstones into brass, copper and/or silver. Create a stone set jewellery project from a choice of simple earrings, a pendant, or a ring.
Students who have already done a Lost Wax Cast Rings class may bring their cast rings and try their hand at setting stones in those instead of starting a new project.
Students will learn ‘Flush Setting’, a method of embedding gems into the surface of metal without the need for solder or joins. The process is adaptable to a number of jewellery types and styles. Students with prior Flush Setting and soldering experience may advance to other methods and to try their hand at learning ‘Bead Setting’, or a simple form of ‘Prong Setting’.
Students may choose from lab-created Sapphires, Rubies and other high strength stones such as Cubic Zirconia in a range of colours.
The workshop is especially satisfying for the magpie in all of us, adding extra colour and shine to jewellery making.
While both beginners and returning students are welcome in the class, be aware that the tools and techniques require a level of fine motor skills, keen eye for detail, and strong hand control which take some patience, practice and perseverance to get the best results. 
Our magnifying headsets, work clamps, and patient tutor will help you along the way. 
Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
By the end of the course, students will be able to:
Develop jewellery making skills with introductory faceted stone setting methods
Create a simple stone set project - either earrings, a pendant, or a ring.
Use Vernier Calipers to measure and calculate metal dimensions
Use files to shape brass, copper and/or silver components
Use a soldering torch to attach metal components such as jump rings
File, sand and polish brass, copper and/or silver creations into finished jewellery
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Men’s Health & Aging Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Men’s Health & Aging Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Men's Health & Aging: Take Charge of Your Future
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Aging is inevitable, but how you age is up to you. This essential course offers men a rare opportunity to confront the physical, mental, and social changes that come with age, with a holistic approach to staying strong and engaged.
What We’ll Cover:
Physical Health:
Stay Ahead: Preventive care strategies to keep you at your best.
Fuel & Fitness: Nutrition and exercise tips tailored for men over 40.
Managing Chronic Conditions: Tackle issues like hypertension and diabetes head-on.
Mental Health:
Stay Sharp: Techniques to manage stress, anxiety, and maintain cognitive health.
Mind & Mood: Discover mindfulness and relaxation for a balanced mind.
Movement & Activity:
Keep Moving: Boost flexibility, balance, and strength to stay active.
Strength Training: Simple routines to maintain muscle and vitality.
Your Role in the World:
Purpose & Identity: Redefine your role and find fulfillment in later life.
Community & Connections: Build lasting relationships and a strong support network.
Balance: Master the art of balancing work, life, and leisure.
Fact vs. Fiction:
Media Myths: Challenge aging stereotypes and embrace a positive outlook.
Real Talk: Get the facts about aging and ditch the myths.
Why Join?
This course isn’t offered everywhere, and it’s more important than ever. Take charge of your health, your life, and your future. It’s time to embrace aging with confidence and resilience.
This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.

events.id: Psychology of Money Management-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Psychology of Money Management
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Psychology of Money Management
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Managing finances should be empowering and enjoyable, yet traditional methods often make money a stressful, taboo topic—even with your closest loved ones. In this workshop, we're not here to tell you to cut your budget or skip your favourite treats. Instead, we’ll come together to design a financial plan tailored to your unique needs, making you feel confident, excited, and in control.

In this workshop, you’ll explore the psychology of money and how it influences your financial decisions. You’ll also learn the basic investing concepts that are essential for building wealth, so you can understand them better when making choices about your future. Through real-life case studies, step-by-step guidance, and collaborative exercises, you’ll design a financial system that suits your lifestyle, reduces stress, and helps you achieve your goals.
If you want to simplify your finances and live without the worry of every dollar spent, then this is the workshop for you!
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Disclaimer:  The tutor is not a financial advisor. No investments, products nor specific recommendations will be provided.  A step-by-step planning process will be taught by working through a case study.   Any product names or websites used in examples and explanations to help participants understand financial concepts and the planning process are used for clarity only. They are not given as any type of recommendation.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. Psychology of Money Management This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.

events.id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Retirement Reinvented
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Retirement Reinvented
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.
It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the day, you’ll have:
Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your values and your strengths
Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explore choices that can provide you with that sense of purpose
Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Begun to develop your blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and have considered a “future proofing” downsizing plan.
Bring pen and paper.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Night Photography - In the Dark-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Night Photography - In the Dark
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Night Photography - In the Dark
This course gives you the opportunity to explore and sharpen both your technical and creative skills, so you have the ability and vision to capture the enchanting world that resides in the darkness.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Identify the equipment needed to take photographs at night
• Learn fundamentals of photography composition and editing of night images
• Identify how to use technical photographic tools in a night time context
• Explore techniques that allow you to develop a more creative approach to picture making including: light painting, intentional camera movement and light trails
• Recognise the importance of light and colour in night photography and develop your ability to use it to make dynamic images
Course includes a couple of evening field trips (tutor will advise of suitable dates once course is under way).
You must bring a tripod and, if you have one, a neutral density filter and cable release remote. You need a fully manual DSLR or mirrorless camera and an intermediate understanding of camera functions. Not suitable for point and shoot cameras.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Brynzilian Big Band-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Brynzilian Big Band
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Brynzilian Big Band is a 17-piece powerhouse jazz orchestra founded and led by saxophonist Bryn van Vliet. Based in Wellington, Bryn is a multi-disciplinary artist known for his dynamic career as an instrumentalist (saxophone, woodwinds, vocals), performer, composer, arranger, and educator. As a founding member of TOI and former lead alto saxophonist for the Rodger Fox Big Band, he has earned a reputation as a versatile and sought-after musician.

The Brynzilian Big Band presents fresh, vibrant compositions inspired by iconic Brazilian music styles such as Samba, Bossa Nova, and Forró. Bryn’s writing reflects his deep affection for Brazilian music, while honoring its traditional roots and spirit. Featuring 17 of Wellington’s finest musicians, the Brynzilian Big Band promises a performance that will make you dance, laugh, shout, cry — and maybe even hug your neighbour.

Music from 9pm, $20 on. the door $10 for students

events.id: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Multi-award-winning Kiwi comic, radio presenter and TV personality Hayley Sproull is back in her hometown. She has a brand-new show that’s not safe for on-air. In Sproull On the Prowl, the self-described walking tornado (over)shares about a wild 12 months. It features stifled farts, hall passes, and an exploration of the Indonesian bush with a cameo from Jesus. 
The Toi Whakaari graduate has come a long way since bursting onto the comedy scene in 2012 when she was named Best Newcomer at that year’s International Comedy Festival. She’s made herself a regular on our airwaves since then through her breakfast radio show and appearances on Taskmaster, 7 Days, Have You Been Paying Attention? and The Great Kiwi Bake Off. Make sure you leave the kids at home for what promises to be a wild night.

Learn the essential skills and techniques needed to make your own creations. The morning will begin with simple design examples and techniques using specialist jewellers’ tools, working with copper or brass. In the afternoon you will form and craft your own ring from sterling silver.

events.id: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $49 with ID, all others pay $120).
Put in the hard work on this one day Saturday workshop and experience the benefits of self-knowledge. Let the emotional knowledge you gain inform not only how you live your life but also how you feel about living it. You’ll be asking the big questions about not only how you live your life, but also why. Discover the tools and scientifically based information to inform the exploration of your life’s purpose.
Consider your emotional needs and how they relate to personal meaning. You’ll unwrap the concepts of emotional nutrition and be shown how to be guided by your values and start to think of them as a compass directing the journey of your life. To do this you’ll identify your values hierarchy and learn about how this framework can inform how you live your life. Once you can apply a spirit of consistency to your self-identified larger purpose, as well as your needs, then your life’s purpose, emotional needs and core values, will align. It’s quite a transformational day!
Bring pen and paper.  Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch/snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Develop a Life Purpose Statement
Perform an Emotional Needs Self-Assessment
Elicit Your Core Values
Align your goals with your life purpose, emotional needs and core values
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
The ultimate class in practical cheese making!   Learn all you need to know to replicate making NZ style Brie and Queso Fresco at home.  You will be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of Brie. Then, while the Brie is ripening and setting (don't worry, all terminology will be explained!), we will make Queso Fresco together.  
All ingredients are provided as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, a dishcloth, 3 or 4 tea towels, a pen to take notes and a 2 litre container and plastic re-sealable food bags to take your cheese home.
During the workshop, the following topics will also be covered: Milk and where to source it, hygiene and sanitation, equipment required and the origins of the cheeses you are about to make.  You will also receive comprehensive instruction on how to mature your Brie cheese over the next few weeks.
A small break will be provided and you will have the opportunity to purchase equipment and cheesemaking ingredients after the workshop should you wish to replicate the experience at home.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Make Brie and Queso Fresco
- Recall the best milk to use and where to source it from
- Name and identify what equipment and ingredients you need to make Brie and Queso Fresco
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
Love Singapore Hawker food? Come and discover how to recreate some of these dishes from local ingredients with Mei-Fen, who is passionate about Asian food. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.   NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Prepare a range of Singapore “hawker” style dishes which may Kueh Dadar, Mee Goreng and Laksa
• Follow the recipes provided and prepare them with supervision
• Prepare the recipes from local ingredients
• Identify retail outlets which supply the specialty ingredients required 
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

events.id: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Meet the demand for excellence in the job market head on. Learn how to utilise the STARR technique in the way that recruiters use it. That is, to define and seek out the skills and qualities of the best candidate. Master the STARR skills and feel confident in your next job interview.

Familiarise yourself with the skills needed to ace your next interview. Learn the STARR method as an approach to responding to behavioural based questions. Understand how to use concrete examples from your life as proof that you possess the experience and skills for the job you are applying for. You’ll develop the key interview skill of being able to share your accomplishments through real-life examples.

In this course, you are taught how to identify specific example situations that are unique to you. You’ll learn how to confidently talk about the tasks involved and describe how you completed the key task and met its challenges. We’ll also consider the context of jobs you are interviewing for and how to choose examples that best show the desired skills interviewers are seeking.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Develop a higher level of confidence in approaching job interviews.
- Identify the different components of the STARR acronym and apply them to the job interview context.
- Identify what behavioural based questions are and why recruiters use them.
- Successfully answer behavioural based interview questions by applying the STARR technique.
- Develop skills in using real life examples to show transferable skills in the workplace.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from ChatGPT
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools like ChatGPT are everywhere, but how do you get the best out of them? In this hands-on, phone-based workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make ChatGPT a more useful, reliable partner in your work and life. Whether you’ve tried it a few times or use it regularly, you’ll learn how to write better prompts, refine responses, and avoid common pitfalls without needing any technical background.
We’ll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together so you leave feeling confident and ready to put your new skills to use.
Please bring: A phone with the ChatGPT app installed, and enough battery to last the day.

events.name: Zine Making - Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zine Making - Introduction
In this workshop you will be guided in making a classic cut and fold 8-page zine from a standard A4 sheet of paper - this is a clever design you can easily photocopy and reproduce later! You will be introduced to a range of techniques and approaches such as cut and paste photos and text, collage and drawing on paper.
If you’re keen to make more zines and share them with the world, there's a broad and growing community of Zine-makers and traders, as well as the well-attended annual Wellington Zinefest!
Paper, newspapers, magazines and basic drawing materials will be provided. We recommend you bring along a sketch book and any favourite drawing materials, newspapers, magazines or other interesting paper material to cut up and collage with in class.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Cut and fold a classic 8-page zine format from a single sheet of paper
Brainstorm content ideas for your personalised zine publications
Arrange & compose text and images to fit a classic 8-page zine layout
Combine cut & paste magazine content with hand drawn/ written content
Create a draft zine of your personal interests
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: C-26
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
First gig for C-26 in 2026 at Rogue and Vagabond. Just $10 at the door or online. We will be there from 9:30 pm. Bring your friends and celebrate with some cool salsa tunes. Spread the love and see you there.

C-26 is a sizzling seven piece salsa band from Wellington. They are a great mix of South American and Kiwi professional musicians who bring vibrant and energetic music to the Wellington scene.

Their sound and style is inspired by the salsa classics from Cuba Puerto Rico and Colombia. Featuring the first female Latino salsa singer in Wellington, Anayibi Loboa from Colombia, C-26 is the Salsa purist's band, fully live, with no backing tracks, C-26 produces a sound and feel built from the classical combination of horns, timbales, congas, guiro, trumpet, saxophone, piano and bass that defines the authentic salsa experience.

Music from 9:30pm, tickets from Hummanitix

events.id: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
THE WATERBOYS make their long-awaited return to New Zealand in May 2026 their first tour in over a decade!
Critically acclaimed as one of the best live bands on the planet, the 2025 band features double keyboard players Brother Paul from Memphis and James Hallawell from Cornwall and is grounded by killer Irish rhythm section Aongus Ralston (bass) and Eamon Ferris (drums).
THE WATERBOYS will be performing tracks from their audacious new album, Life, Death and Dennis Hopper: a bold, cinematic tribute to the maverick American actor and cultural icon, along with material across their storied catalogue. From the early "big music" of The Whole Of The Moon and classic album This Is The Sea through to the roots-inspired Fisherman's Blues era and music from their ambitious post-2015 output.
Mike Scott comments: "The Waterboys live for the experience of playing, of feeling our music evolve and grow with each consecutive concert. Nothing makes us happier than seeing a long list of dates like these. How will the music change? What will happen that's never happened before? How much fun and thrills can we and the audiences have? Australia and New Zealand come and join us!"

events.id: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s a world-renowned guitarist and master entertainer. Tommy Emmanuel, CGP, is bringing his beloved Maton guitar to The Opera House stage in Wellington. He is considered a living legend in the acoustic guitar world. The Australian musical legend has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling technique. The concert promises to be an inspiring journey as Tommy strums and plucks his way through jazz, rock, country and pop music. Fans will be captivated by the diverse musical landscape he creates. 
Since his career began in the 1960s, Tommy has toured with luminaries like Eric Clapton and John Denver. He has won a Grammy Award and performed for a televised audience of more than two billion at the 2000 Sydney Olympics. Collaborations include guitar greats like Les Paul, Mark Knopfler, Joe Walsh, and Richard Thompson. 
Tommy’s hero, legendary guitarist Chet Atkins, bestowed the title of Certified Guitar Player (CGP) on him. He is one of only four players in the world with the title. Tommy Emmanuel CGP continues to push the boundaries of what is possible on the acoustic guitar.

events.id: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Michael Fowler Centre is set for two visits by the alt-country/folk musician and his band, The Yarra Benders. The tour is called Tā te Manawa — it literally means ‘heart at rest’. This references Marlon’s wish to take a break after a particularly busy and productive few years. 
Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening. The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’. The second delves into repertoire from his first album and bits of everything in between. Marlon explains the reason for the break, “For nearly 20 years I’ve explored both the physical and musical world in the company of incredible musicians, songwriters and friends. The catch with it is — it can be easy to overlook the toll it can take over time, on body and soul.” Fair not though, this is only a hiatus. Marlon describes it as a cheerful hurrah, “a see you soon of sorts.”

Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening.
The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’.

events.id: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for some of the biggest anthems in the rock world. Rock Tenors roar into The Opera House in Wellington for a night of big tunes and big voices. The Anthems Reloaded Tour celebrates key moments in the Kiwi group’s musical journey. 
The five members of Rock Tenors have wide experience. They cross multiple music genres from opera and musical theatre to contemporary rock and thrash metal. They’ve reinvented the standard rock vocal arrangements with harmonic complexity, so audiences will get a dose of skilled musicality as well as nostalgia. You’ll hear The Rock Tenors’ signature renditions of legendary anthems from bands stretching back to the 60s. Hear Queen, The Animals, Bon Jovi, Aerosmith, AC/DC, Black Sabbath, Iron Maiden, White Snake, KISS, Guns N’ Roses, Red Hot Chilli Peppers, U2, and more. 
The touring ensemble of James Adams, Luke Butson, Ben Hayward, Steve Jones, and Justin Wilson are some of New Zealand’s premier vocalists. Since forming in 2022, they have taken the South Island by storm. They have become the must-see act for their vocal mastery and high-voltage rock singing.

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: Strategy, Skills and Self-Awareness
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Equip yourself with the tools and strategies to thrive in today’s competitive job market. This course is designed to help job seekers at all stages across any industry. Whether you're applying for your first job, changing careers, or re-entering the workforce, this course is relevant for everyone because it requires you to self-reflect on your own journey. You’ll learn to reflect deeply on your goals and strengths while building practical skills to stand out from the crowd.
 You’ll leave with a set of practical tools, clear strategies, and a better understanding of what employers want, so you can step into your next opportunity with confidence.
 You’ll develop a tailored job search strategy that works for you. One that has purpose, highlights your transferable skills, sharpens your professional image, and helps you connect authentically with hiring managers. From crafting a digital-ready CV and setting job alerts, to working with recruiters and networking effectively, you’ll gain tools and insights to streamline the process.
 The course also addresses the emotional journey of job hunting. Learn to manage time and energy, stay adaptable, and build resilience as you face the highs and lows of searching for the right opportunity. You’ll explore how emotional intelligence, digital literacy, and critical thinking play essential roles in first impressions, interviews, and long-term career decisions.
 By the end of this course, you’ll be able to:
Identify and articulate your transferable skills.
Apply both emotional and intellectual strategies to mentor and coach yourself from within.
Develop a job search strategy that aligns with your goals.
Build confidence in presenting yourself professionally and authentically.
Develop a confident, authentic approach to your personal branding and professional networking.
Create and manage a job search plan using shortcuts like job alerts, recruitment agents, and focussed applications.
Maintain a polished digital presence and decide whether the use of professional platforms are for you (LinkedIn).
Understand how to navigate the emotional demands of job hunting with self-compassion, motivation, and adaptability.
Build and maintain a network of trusted referees who reflect your work ethic and potential.
 Whether you're starting from scratch or just need a refresh, this course offers a structured, encouraging path forward to help you land your next opportunity.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
Join us for a fun day of creature making! Design and sculpt a one-of-a-kind character in your own style from polymer clays in a full spectrum of colours!

Working with oven-cured clays and wrapped wire armatures, this workshop offers a variety of modelling methods and approaches - a great opportunity to turn sketches & ideas into tangible 3D forms. Modern clay inventions mean we can create translucent, glittery, semi-flexible, UV reactive or even glow-in-the-dark elements to add more realism or a touch of the surreal into each creation!

Students will be coached through designing and creating a small-scale creature sculpture, beginning simple, and building to a more complex final form as we get familiar with materials.

The class offers beginner students a fun introduction to sculpting organic forms from scratch, and for those with some hands-on creative experience, it offers a great opportunity to experiment, hone and advance your making skills to new levels.

If you can, bring along some design ideas, sketches or reference images to help kick start the sculpting process on the day.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
An interactive workshop presented by experienced physiotherapist Sara Dore, focusing on how movement and function can be challenging for women as they age. Particularly for women, there is more to the core than abdominal muscles and strength and we explore these broader aspects.  Most importantly, you will come to understand that movement is medicine and we can make a difference.
You will be able to make a positive difference for yourself! The objective of this workshop is to give you knowledge and understanding about how you can manage the effects on your health and wellbeing as we age as women.  Knowledge is power!
In this one-day workshop you will discover:
- The base of the pelvic floor. Anatomy of the pelvic floor, and the common conditions urinary incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse will be discussed. Education on basic pelvic floor advice and management as well as appropriate exercises will also be covered.
- The middle deep abdominal muscles. Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed. The principles of Clinical Pilates versus traditional Pilates will be presented together with the key principles of movement is medicine.
-The importance of the diaphragm and breathing well will also be discussed to enhance optimal movement and function for women.
Please wear comfortable clothes and socks as this is an interactive workshop, and bring a yoga mat or beach towel.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Writing Short Stories
In this one-day course, we’ll explore the elements that make short stories shine, from creating compelling character dynamics, to choosing the point of view that best serves your story.
Through a mix of discussions and practical writing exercises, participants will leave the course with all the tools they need to write engaging short stories.
Please bring pen and paper, and the name of a short story you like.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring your lunch and snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify elements that make short stories memorable
- Create compelling characters and relationship dynamics
- Write using different points of view
- Explore using symbolism in short fiction
- Use detail to develop setting and character traits
- Identify places to send your completed short stories
